fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized maintaining a minimum garrison, customs service, and port operation while preserving the colonial foothold at low cost.

description.zh: 负责据点驻守、巡逻和对周边乡村施压。
description.en: Responsible for post defense, patrols, and coercive operations around surrounding rural zones.

description.zh: 维持比绍及贸易点秩序，协助税收与缉私。
description.en: Maintained order in Bissau and trading points and assisted with taxation and anti-smuggling.

description.zh: 执行河口巡逻、交通护卫和有限海防任务。
description.en: Conducted estuary patrols, transport escort, and limited coastal defense duties.

commanderInChief.zh: 葡属几内亚总督兼驻军最高长官
commanderInChief.en: Governor of Portuguese Guinea as head of the garrison

technology.zh: 装备以步枪、小型炮和少数巡逻艇为主，通信与医疗条件较差，作战能力局限于沿海和河口。
technology.en: Equipment consisted mainly of rifles, small artillery pieces, and a few patrol boats, with poor communications and medical support; combat capacity was largely confined to the coast and estuaries.

threats.zh: 主要威胁为地方社群抵抗、热带疾病、走私活动和内陆控制无力。
threats.en: Main threats were local communal resistance, tropical disease, smuggling, and inability to control the interior.

populationDescription.zh: 人口由巴兰特、富拉、曼丁卡、帕佩尔等多个族群组成，宗教与习俗多元。城市人口很少，多数居民分布在河流、稻作区和乡村聚落。
populationDescription.en: The population consisted of many groups including the Balanta, Fula, Mandinka, and Papel, with diverse religions and customs. Urban population was very small, and most inhabitants lived along rivers, in rice-growing zones, and in rural settlements.

socialClasses.zh: 殖民官员与商人、小规模混合中间群体、地方首领、自由农民、渔民、手工业者与季节性劳工。
socialClasses.en: Colonial officials and merchants, a small mixed intermediary stratum, local chiefs, free cultivators, fishers, artisans, and seasonal laborers.

allies.zh: 宗主国葡萄牙，并在西非海上秩序中与其他欧洲殖民势力保持有限合作。
allies.en: Metropolitan Portugal, with limited cooperation with other European colonial powers in West African maritime order.

enemies.zh: 无明确主权敌国，主要面对地方抵抗和与邻近殖民边界相关的摩擦。
enemies.en: There was no clear sovereign enemy state; the colony mainly faced local resistance and frictions related to neighboring colonial frontiers.

foreignPolicy.zh: 其外交完全服从葡萄牙，重点在维持沿海据点、河口贸易安全与边界承认。
foreignPolicy.en: Its foreign policy was entirely subordinate to Portugal, focusing on holding coastal posts, securing estuarine trade, and preserving frontier recognition.

era.zh: 工业时代晚期边缘殖民据点
era.en: Late Industrial Age peripheral colonial outpost

keyInnovations.zh: 蒸汽航运、小型河口巡逻艇、电报有限延伸、后装步枪与港口仓储。
keyInnovations.en: Steam shipping, small estuarine patrol boats, limited telegraph extension, breech-loading rifles, and port warehousing.

strengths.zh: 沿海和河口贸易位置有利，行政规模小，维持殖民据点成本相对可控。
strengths.en: Its coastal and estuarine trade position was favorable, and the colony's small administrative scale made the cost of maintaining outposts relatively manageable.

weaknesses.zh: 财政极其脆弱、人口与市场规模有限、内陆控制薄弱且高度依赖海运。
weaknesses.en: Finances were extremely fragile, population and market size were limited, inland control was weak, and the colony was highly dependent on sea transport.

outlook.zh: 短期内将继续作为低投入、小规模出口型殖民地存在，若无重大基础设施投资，整体发展空间有限。
outlook.en: In the short term it would likely continue as a low-investment, small-scale export colony, with limited prospects absent major infrastructure investment.

description.zh: 1900年前后的葡属几内亚是一个以比绍和河口贸易为中心、行政与财政都十分有限的葡萄牙西非殖民地。
description.en: Around 1900, Portuguese Guinea was a Portuguese West African colony centered on Bissau and estuarine trade, with very limited administrative and fiscal capacity.

dynasty.en: Colonial Rule of the Kingdom of the Netherlands

governmentForm.zh: 荷属圭亚那在1900年前后是由荷兰王国任命总督管理的殖民地，地方政务受海牙殖民政策与殖民议会框架共同影响。
governmentForm.en: Around 1900, Dutch Guiana was a colony governed by a governor appointed by the Kingdom of the Netherlands, with local administration shaped by both metropolitan colonial policy and colonial council structures.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由欧洲裔殖民官员与商人、解放后的非洲裔克里奥尔居民、英属印度契约劳工、爪哇移民、犹太社群以及内陆的马龙人与美洲原住民组成。沿海种植园与帕拉马里博形成较集中的殖民社会，而内陆地区行政控制较弱。种族、法律地位与职业分工对社会层级影响显著。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of European colonial officials and merchants, emancipated Afro-Surinamese Creoles, indentured laborers from British India, Javanese migrants, Jewish communities, and inland Maroon and Indigenous peoples. Coastal plantations and Paramaribo formed the core of colonial society, while the interior remained only loosely administered. Ethnicity, legal status, and occupation strongly shaped social hierarchy.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要是荷兰任命的总督、高级殖民官员、种植园主与城市商人。地方精英在财政与市政事务上有一定影响，但主权权力仍掌握在宗主国手中。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was composed mainly of the Dutch-appointed governor, senior colonial officials, plantation owners, and urban merchants. Local elites held some influence in fiscal and municipal affairs, but sovereign authority remained in metropolitan hands.

succession.zh: 殖民地最高权力并非世袭，而由荷兰政府任命总督接替。重要行政职位通常由宗主国调派或核准任命。
succession.en: The colony’s highest authority was not hereditary but passed through gubernatorial appointment by the Dutch government. Major administrative posts were usually assigned or approved by the metropole.

structure.zh: 总督为殖民地行政首脑，下设秘书处、财政与税务机关、司法机关及公共工程系统。殖民地存在顾问或议政机构，但其权限有限，关键政策由总督与荷兰殖民部主导。沿海区行政较为成体系，内陆多依赖驻点、警察与间接管理。
structure.en: The governor served as the colony’s chief executive, assisted by a secretariat, fiscal and revenue offices, judicial institutions, and a public works apparatus. Advisory or deliberative colonial bodies existed but had limited powers, with major policy directed by the governor and the Dutch colonial ministry. Coastal districts were more systematically governed, while the interior relied on posts, police, and indirect administration.

function.zh: 负责政令发布、人事、档案、与海牙的行政往来。
function.en: Handled decrees, personnel, records, and official correspondence with The Hague.

function.zh: 负责关税、消费税、土地与出口税征收，以及殖民地预算编制。
function.en: Managed customs, excise, land and export taxes, and prepared the colonial budget.

function.zh: 负责法院运作、治安、监狱与劳工监管事务。
function.en: Oversaw courts, policing, prisons, and labor regulation.

name.en: Department of Public Works and Colonial Estates

function.zh: 负责道路、港口、排水、防洪、官地与基础设施维护。
function.en: Supervised roads, port facilities, drainage, flood control, state lands, and infrastructure maintenance.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以帕拉马里博及沿海区划为核心，由区官与警务人员管理。内陆马龙人与原住民地区多采取有限接触和间接控制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on Paramaribo and the coastal districts, managed by district officers and police personnel. Inland Maroon and Indigenous areas were often handled through limited contact and indirect control.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以荷兰殖民法和成文法传统为基础，并结合地方法院实施。劳工契约、土地权属与刑事治安是司法重点。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on Dutch colonial law and civil law traditions, administered through local courts. Labor contracts, land tenure, and criminal order were among its chief concerns.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要来自进出口关税、消费税、许可证费与种植园相关税项。由于经济规模有限，财政对贸易税和宗主国支持较为敏感。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied chiefly on import-export duties, excises, license fees, and plantation-related levies. Because of the small economy, public finance was highly sensitive to trade taxes and metropolitan support.

religion.zh: 基督教
religion.en: Christianity

culturalAchievements.zh: 帕拉马里博形成了独特的木构殖民城市景观，并融合荷兰、非洲、犹太与亚洲移民传统。宗教教育、印刷出版和多语社会生活在沿海地区较为突出。
culturalAchievements.en: Paramaribo developed a distinctive wooden colonial urban landscape blending Dutch, African, Jewish, and Asian migrant traditions. Religious schooling, print culture, and multilingual social life were particularly notable in the coastal zone.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系为官方核心，并存克里奥尔语、南亚语与南岛语成分
languageFamily.en: Indo-European languages formed the official core, alongside creole, South Asian, and Austronesian linguistic elements

philosophy.zh: 以荷兰殖民行政文化、基督教伦理与多族群实用主义并存为特征。
philosophy.en: Characterized by Dutch colonial administrative culture, Christian ethics, and a pragmatic multiethnic social outlook.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用拉丁字母，行政语言为荷兰语，民间亦使用斯拉南通戈等语言。
writingSystem.en: Used the Latin alphabet; Dutch was the administrative language, while Sranan Tongo and other languages were widely used in society.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模较小，以出口型种植园和港口贸易为主。糖、可可、咖啡与金矿开采构成主要收入来源，但波动较大。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was small and centered on export plantations and port trade. Sugar, cocoa, coffee, and gold mining provided the main sources of income, though they were volatile.

mainIndustries.zh: 甘蔗种植、可可与咖啡、黄金开采、木材采伐、港口贸易
mainIndustries.en: Sugar cultivation, cocoa and coffee, gold mining, timber extraction, port commerce

tradeGoods.zh: 糖、糖蜜、可可、咖啡、黄金、木材、进口制成品与纺织品
tradeGoods.en: Sugar, molasses, cocoa, coffee, gold, timber, imported manufactures and textiles

householdWealth.zh: 城市商人和种植园主财富较高，多数劳工与小农资产有限，家庭财富差距明显。
householdWealth.en: Urban merchants and plantation owners held comparatively high wealth, while most laborers and small cultivators had limited assets, producing marked household inequality.

economicSystem.zh: 殖民地出口导向经济，依赖种植园劳动、港口转运和宗主国贸易网络。
economicSystem.en: A colonial export-oriented economy dependent on plantation labor, port transshipment, and metropolitan trade networks.

treasuryDescription.zh: 殖民地库藏规模有限，通常只足以应付短期财政波动与季节性支出。
treasuryDescription.en: The colonial treasury was modest, generally sufficient only for short-term fiscal fluctuations and seasonal expenditures.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，重视平衡预算与维持港口、警务和排水等基本殖民治理功能。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative, emphasizing budget balance and the maintenance of basic colonial functions such as ports, policing, and drainage.

description.zh: 负责首府、防御据点、仓库与沿海治安。
description.en: Responsible for the capital, defensive posts, depots, and coastal security.

description.zh: 执行巡逻、劳工管制、河道通行检查与地方秩序维持。
description.en: Conducted patrols, labor supervision, riverine checks, and maintenance of local order.

description.zh: 依托小型炮艇和港务设施，负责河口与内河交通控制。
description.en: Used small gunboats and port facilities to control estuarine and inland water traffic.

commanderInChief.zh: 荷属圭亚那总督

technology.zh: 装备以十九世纪末步枪、小型火炮、炮艇和电报通信为主，整体水平符合中小型殖民地守备需要。
technology.en: Equipment centered on late nineteenth-century rifles, light artillery, gunboats, and telegraph communications, adequate for a small colonial garrison.

threats.zh: 主要威胁并非大规模外敌入侵，而是内陆控制薄弱、走私、边界不清及局部社会动荡。
threats.en: The main threats were not major foreign invasion but weak interior control, smuggling, unclear frontiers, and localized social unrest.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约8万，分布高度不均，绝大多数集中于首府及沿海种植园地带。族群构成复杂，包含克里奥尔人、印度契约劳工、爪哇人、欧洲人、犹太人、马龙人与原住民。
populationDescription.en: The population was about 80,000 and very unevenly distributed, with most inhabitants concentrated in the capital and coastal plantation belt. It was ethnically diverse, including Creoles, Indian indentured laborers, Javanese, Europeans, Jews, Maroons, and Indigenous peoples.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为殖民官员、种植园主与商人；中层包括职员、店主、手工业者；下层主要是契约劳工、码头工人、小农与内陆社群。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of colonial officials, plantation owners, and merchants; the middle included clerks, shopkeepers, and artisans; the lower ranks were mainly indentured laborers, dockworkers, small cultivators, and interior communities.

allies.zh: 荷兰王国及其殖民网络；与邻近英法殖民地保持务实边境合作。
allies.en: The Kingdom of the Netherlands and its colonial network; pragmatic border cooperation with neighboring British and French colonies.

enemies.zh: 无正式敌国，主要摩擦来自边界、走私与区域竞争。
enemies.en: No formal enemy state; main frictions arose from borders, smuggling, and regional competition.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策完全服从荷兰，重点在维持边界稳定、保障贸易与避免卷入加勒比大国冲突。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was entirely subordinate to the Netherlands, focusing on frontier stability, trade protection, and avoidance of entanglement in wider Caribbean power conflicts.

era.zh: 第二次工业革命时代的边缘殖民地
era.en: Peripheral colony in the age of the Second Industrial Revolution

keyInnovations.zh: 蒸汽航运、电报、现代港务设施、改良排水与热带种植园管理技术。
keyInnovations.en: Steam navigation, telegraphy, modern port facilities, improved drainage, and tropical plantation management techniques.

strengths.zh: 港口与沿海种植园体系稳定，多族群劳动力来源丰富，与荷兰贸易联系紧密。
strengths.en: Stable port and coastal plantation system, diverse labor sources, and strong commercial ties to the Netherlands.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少、市场狭小、内陆控制弱，对单一出口和外部价格变化依赖较强。
weaknesses.en: Small population, narrow domestic market, weak interior control, and heavy dependence on a few exports and external price shifts.

outlook.zh: 短期内仍将维持稳定殖民地地位，但长期发展取决于劳工制度调整、基础设施扩展与资源开发成效。
outlook.en: It was likely to remain a stable colony in the short term, but long-term development depended on labor-system adjustment, infrastructure expansion, and successful resource exploitation.

description.zh: 荷属圭亚那是一个人口稀少、经济以种植园和港口贸易为核心的荷兰热带殖民地。
description.en: Dutch Guiana was a sparsely populated Dutch tropical colony whose economy centered on plantations and port trade.

dynasty.en: Colonial Rule of the British Empire

governmentForm.zh: 英属圭亚那在1900年前后是由英国王室主权下的直辖殖民地，总督代表伦敦统治，并依赖殖民地官僚与有限代议机构治理。
governmentForm.en: Around 1900, British Guiana was a Crown colony under British sovereignty, ruled by a governor representing London and administered through a colonial bureaucracy with limited representative institutions.

socialStructure.zh: 社会结构由英国殖民官员、欧洲与本地种植园精英、非洲裔克里奥尔居民、印度契约劳工及其后裔、葡萄牙商人、华人和原住民共同构成。海岸低地与糖业地区人口最密集，内陆广大地区开发较少。种植园劳动制度和族群分工是社会秩序的重要基础。
socialStructure.en: The social structure included British colonial officials, European and local plantation elites, Afro-Creole residents, Indian indentured laborers and their descendants, Portuguese merchants, Chinese migrants, and Indigenous peoples. The coastal lowlands and sugar districts were the most densely populated, while the vast interior remained lightly developed. Plantation labor systems and ethnic occupational divisions were central to social order.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由总督、殖民地高级文官、司法人员、糖业种植园主和商业寡头组成。尽管存在地方议政传统，真正决定权仍归属殖民行政与英国政府。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the governor, senior colonial civil servants, judicial officials, sugar planters, and commercial oligarchs. Despite local deliberative traditions, real authority remained with the colonial administration and the British government.

succession.zh: 最高统治权通过英国政府任命总督实现更替，不实行本地世袭。殖民地主要职位由英方委任，并受殖民部监督。
succession.en: Supreme authority changed through British appointment of governors rather than local heredity. Major colonial offices were filled by imperial appointment under Colonial Office supervision.

structure.zh: 总督居于行政体系顶端，下设殖民秘书处、财政机关、司法与警务、公共工程与移民劳工管理机构。殖民地保留政策法院和若干代议性机构，但选举权受财产与种族结构间接限制。行政重点集中于海岸排水、糖业生产、治安和劳工控制。
structure.en: The governor stood at the apex of administration, supported by a colonial secretariat, finance offices, justice and police bodies, and public works and immigration-labor agencies. The colony retained the Court of Policy and related representative institutions, though franchise and influence were constrained by property and social structure. Administration focused on coastal drainage, sugar production, public order, and labor control.

function.zh: 负责总督行政、法规、档案与对伦敦的往来。
function.en: Handled gubernatorial administration, regulations, archives, and correspondence with London.

function.zh: 负责关税、土地税、印花税、殖民预算和贸易监管。
function.en: Managed customs, land taxes, stamp duties, the colonial budget, and trade regulation.

function.zh: 负责法院、监狱、治安巡逻、刑事侦缉与劳工秩序。
function.en: Oversaw courts, prisons, policing, criminal investigation, and labor order.

name.en: Department of Public Works and Immigration

function.zh: 负责堤坝、运河、道路、港口、排水系统及契约劳工事务。
function.en: Supervised sea defenses, canals, roads, ports, drainage systems, and indentured labor affairs.

localAdmin.zh: 地方以县区和种植园区域为管理单元，警察、治安法官与区官承担日常治理。内陆地区主要依赖小型驻点和探勘机构维持象征性控制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through counties, districts, and plantation areas, with police, magistrates, and district officers handling daily governance. The interior was controlled more symbolically through small posts and surveying or mining agencies.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系源自英国殖民法与罗马-荷兰法混合传统，反映该地荷兰旧制遗留。商法、土地法、劳工条例与刑法在殖民治理中尤为重要。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined British colonial law with a Roman-Dutch legal heritage, reflecting the territory’s earlier Dutch institutions. Commercial law, land law, labor ordinances, and criminal law were especially important in governance.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要依赖进出口关税、消费税、土地和财产税、印花税及各类许可证收费。财政基础与糖业出口及海运贸易紧密相连。
taxationSystem.en: Tax revenue depended mainly on import-export duties, excises, land and property taxes, stamp duties, and licensing fees. The fiscal base was closely tied to sugar exports and maritime trade.

religion.zh: 基督教
religion.en: Christianity

culturalAchievements.zh: 乔治敦形成具有殖民木建筑、运河与公共机构特色的城市文化。报刊、教会学校和多族群节庆使该地成为加勒比英属殖民地中较具文化活力的社会之一。
culturalAchievements.en: Georgetown developed an urban culture distinguished by colonial wooden architecture, canals, and public institutions. Newspapers, church schools, and multiethnic festivals made the colony one of the more culturally vibrant societies in the British Caribbean.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系占主导，并有英语克里奥尔及南亚语言成分
languageFamily.en: Predominantly Indo-European, with English creole and South Asian linguistic elements

philosophy.zh: 以英国殖民自由主义、基督教传教价值和多族群实用共处为主要特征。
philosophy.en: Marked by British colonial liberalism, Christian missionary values, and pragmatic coexistence among diverse communities.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用拉丁字母，官方与教育语言为英语，社会中并存克里奥尔英语、印地语方言及其他族群语言。
writingSystem.en: The Latin alphabet was used; English served as the official and educational language, alongside Creole English, Hindustani varieties, and other community languages.

gdpDescription.zh: 英属圭亚那拥有圭亚那三地中最大且最成熟的殖民经济，以糖业为核心，并辅以稻米、林业、金矿和港口服务。其经济对世界糖价、运费与帝国内部市场波动高度敏感。
gdpDescription.en: British Guiana had the largest and most developed colonial economy among the three Guianas, centered on sugar and supplemented by rice, forestry, gold mining, and port services. It was highly sensitive to world sugar prices, freight costs, and fluctuations within imperial markets.

mainIndustries.zh: 糖业、稻米、朗姆酒、黄金开采、林业、港口与航运服务
mainIndustries.en: Sugar, rice, rum, gold mining, forestry, port and shipping services

tradeGoods.zh: 糖、糖蜜、朗姆酒、稻米、黄金、木材、进口机械、纺织品和食品
tradeGoods.en: Sugar, molasses, rum, rice, gold, timber, imported machinery, textiles, and foodstuffs

householdWealth.zh: 种植园资本和城市商业财富集中于少数欧洲及部分移民商人家庭，普通工人与农村家庭储蓄有限，但部分印度裔小农已开始积累土地与牲畜资产。
householdWealth.en: Plantation capital and urban commercial wealth were concentrated among a small number of European and some migrant merchant families, while ordinary workers and rural households had limited savings; however, some Indo-Guyanese smallholders were beginning to accumulate land and livestock.

economicSystem.zh: 典型的英帝国殖民出口经济，由大型糖业企业、契约劳工制度残余和港口贸易共同支撑。
economicSystem.en: A typical British imperial export economy supported by large sugar enterprises, the remnants of indentured labor arrangements, and port-based trade.

source.zh: 港务、许可证及杂项收入

treasuryDescription.zh: 殖民地库藏状况相对稳健，但遇到糖价下跌、洪涝或大型工程时仍可能迅速承压。
treasuryDescription.en: The colonial treasury was relatively sound, though it could come under rapid pressure during sugar price declines, flooding, or major engineering works.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调维持信用、支持排水防洪与港口设施，并尽量避免因社会福利扩张而增加殖民预算负担。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized maintaining credit, supporting drainage, flood control, and port infrastructure, while avoiding heavy budget expansion through broader social welfare commitments.

description.zh: 负责乔治敦、海岸据点、防务设施与关键糖业区安全。
description.en: Protected Georgetown, coastal posts, defense sites, and key sugar districts.

description.zh: 承担治安巡逻、镇压骚乱、边区执法与劳工控制任务。
description.en: Handled patrols, riot suppression, frontier enforcement, and labor control.

description.zh: 装备小型炮艇、岸炮和港防火力，主要用于河口与港湾防御。
description.en: Equipped with small gunboats, coastal guns, and harbor defenses, mainly for estuarine and port protection.

commanderInChief.zh: 英属圭亚那总督

technology.zh: 军备水平与英帝国二线殖民地相当，使用后膛步枪、机关枪、轻炮、蒸汽船与电报系统。
technology.en: Its military level was comparable to that of a second-tier imperial colony, employing breech-loading rifles, machine guns, light artillery, steam craft, and telegraph systems.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自社会骚动、劳工冲突、边界争议余波以及内陆地区治安和走私问题，而非正面大规模战争。
threats.en: The principal threats came from social unrest, labor disputes, aftereffects of boundary controversies, and interior policing and smuggling problems rather than full-scale war.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约30万，是圭亚那地区人口最多的殖民地。人口集中于海岸糖业带和乔治敦，内陆则多为稀疏聚落、矿区和原住民地区。
populationDescription.en: With roughly 300,000 inhabitants, it was the most populous of the Guianas. Population clustered along the coastal sugar belt and in Georgetown, while the interior consisted mainly of sparse settlements, mining zones, and Indigenous areas.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为殖民官僚、种植园主、律师和大商人；中层包括职员、教师、店主、自由小农；下层则为田间劳工、码头工人、仆役和内陆矿工。
socialClasses.en: The upper class comprised colonial bureaucrats, plantation owners, lawyers, and major merchants; the middle included clerks, teachers, shopkeepers, and free smallholders; the lower strata consisted of field laborers, dockworkers, servants, and interior miners.

allies.zh: 大英帝国及其加勒比殖民体系；与邻近英法荷殖民地维持边务与贸易合作。
allies.en: The British Empire and its Caribbean colonial system; maintained frontier and trade cooperation with neighboring British, French, and Dutch colonies.

enemies.zh: 无固定敌国，但与委内瑞拉的边界问题在世纪之交仍留有政治余波。
enemies.en: No fixed enemy state, though the boundary dispute with Venezuela left political aftereffects around the turn of the century.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外事务完全由英国主导，重点在维护边界仲裁成果、保障海运贸易和维持加勒比地区力量平衡。
foreignPolicy.en: External affairs were entirely directed by Britain, with priorities of preserving boundary arbitration outcomes, securing maritime trade, and maintaining the Caribbean balance of power.

era.zh: 第二次工业革命下的成熟热带殖民地经济
era.en: A mature tropical colonial economy in the Second Industrial Revolution

keyInnovations.zh: 现代糖厂蒸汽机械、运河排水工程、电报、海港装卸设施与金矿勘探技术。
keyInnovations.en: Modern steam-powered sugar mills, canal drainage engineering, telegraphy, harbor-handling facilities, and gold prospecting techniques.

strengths.zh: 人口与财政基础较强，糖业和港口体系成熟，英国帝国市场与航运网络提供稳定支撑。
strengths.en: It possessed a stronger demographic and fiscal base, a mature sugar and port system, and stable backing from British imperial markets and shipping networks.

weaknesses.zh: 经济对糖业依赖过深，社会族群分层明显，海岸低地对排水和工程维护高度依赖。
weaknesses.en: The economy was overly dependent on sugar, society was sharply segmented by ethnicity, and the coastal lowlands were highly dependent on drainage and engineering maintenance.

outlook.zh: 若国际糖价保持可接受水平，殖民地前景相对稳健；但劳工政治、民族关系与产业单一化将在未来加剧治理压力。
outlook.en: If world sugar prices remained acceptable, the colony’s prospects were relatively stable; however, labor politics, ethnic relations, and economic monoculture would intensify future governance pressures.

description.zh: 英属圭亚那是英国在南美北岸最重要的糖业殖民地之一，拥有较大人口、较强财政和复杂多族群社会。
description.en: British Guiana was one of Britain’s most important sugar colonies on the northern coast of South America, with a larger population, stronger finances, and a complex multiethnic society.

dynasty.zh: 法兰西第三共和国殖民统治
dynasty.en: Colonial Rule of the French Third Republic

governmentForm.zh: 法属圭亚那在1900年前后是法兰西第三共和国下的殖民地，由法国任命总督或高级行政长官统治，并受巴黎殖民部直接监督。
governmentForm.en: Around 1900, French Guiana was a colony of the French Third Republic, governed by a French-appointed governor or senior administrator under the direct supervision of the ministry in Paris.

socialStructure.zh: 社会规模小而分散，由法国官员、克里奥尔居民、自由黑人后裔、少量欧洲移民、流放犯与刑罚殖民体系人员、以及内陆原住民和马龙社群组成。卡宴及沿海狭小地带是行政和商业中心。刑罚殖民地制度深刻影响了劳动力、社会声望和居民流动。
socialStructure.en: Society was small and dispersed, composed of French officials, Creole residents, descendants of freed blacks, a small number of European settlers, transported convicts and penal personnel, and inland Indigenous and Maroon communities. Cayenne and the narrow coastal strip formed the administrative and commercial core. The penal colony system strongly shaped labor, social standing, and population movement.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要是法国殖民官员、军警人员、司法官与少数商业精英。由于自由移民和本地资本薄弱，国家行政机器在社会中的比重远高于普通殖民地。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted chiefly of French colonial officials, military-police personnel, judicial officers, and a small commercial elite. Because free settlement and local capital were weak, the state administrative apparatus loomed larger than in most colonies.

succession.zh: 最高权力通过法国政府任命更替，不存在本地世袭传统。关键职位受巴黎控制，体现高度中央集权的殖民管理。
succession.en: Supreme authority changed through appointment by the French government, with no local hereditary tradition. Key offices were controlled from Paris, reflecting highly centralized colonial management.

structure.zh: 殖民地行政以总督及其秘书处为核心，下辖财政机关、司法警务机构、刑罚殖民管理系统与公共工程部门。由于人口稀少和刑罚流放性质，行政管理往往兼具普通殖民治理与监禁监管双重功能。沿海城镇受直接管理，内陆地区控制能力有限。
structure.en: Colonial administration centered on the governor and his secretariat, with subordinate finance offices, justice and police institutions, penal colony administration, and public works. Owing to the small population and penal-transport character of the colony, governance combined ordinary colonial administration with custodial supervision. Coastal towns were directly managed, while interior control remained limited.

function.zh: 负责行政命令、对巴黎报告、人事与档案管理。
function.en: Handled administrative orders, reporting to Paris, personnel matters, and records.

function.zh: 负责关税、预算、仓储、税费征收与殖民地采购。
function.en: Managed customs, budgeting, warehousing, tax collection, and colonial procurement.

name.zh: 司法、警务与刑罚殖民局
name.en: Department of Justice, Police, and Penal Colonies

function.zh: 负责法院、监狱、流放犯管理、治安与海岛刑场运行。
function.en: Oversaw courts, prisons, convict administration, public order, and the operation of island penal sites.

name.en: Department of Public Works and Health

function.zh: 负责道路、码头、医院、卫生防疫及政府建筑维护。
function.en: Supervised roads, wharves, hospitals, sanitary measures, and maintenance of government buildings.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以卡宴及少数沿海据点为主，许多偏远地区依靠军警哨所和惩役设施维持存在。内陆原住民区域多处于名义主权之下而非充分行政整合之中。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on Cayenne and a few coastal outposts, while many remote areas were maintained through police-military posts and penal establishments. Interior Indigenous regions remained under nominal sovereignty rather than full administrative integration.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系基于法国成文法传统，同时适用殖民特别条例和刑罚流放规章。普通民政司法与刑罚管理密切交织，是法属圭亚那制度的显著特征。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on French civil law traditions, supplemented by colonial special ordinances and penal transportation regulations. Ordinary civil administration and penal supervision were closely intertwined, a defining feature of French Guiana.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要来自关税、消费税、许可证和部分国家经营收入。由于经济基础狭弱，财政在很大程度上依赖法国本土拨款。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation drew mainly from customs duties, excises, licenses, and some state operating revenues. Because the economic base was narrow, finances depended heavily on transfers from metropolitan France.

religion.zh: 天主教
religion.en: Catholicism

culturalAchievements.zh: 卡宴虽规模不大，但形成了法属加勒比世界中的独特克里奥尔城市文化。宗教节庆、学校教育和法语行政文化在沿海地带占主导，而刑罚殖民地也给文学与公众想象留下强烈印记。
culturalAchievements.en: Though small, Cayenne developed a distinctive Creole urban culture within the French Caribbean world. Religious festivals, schooling, and French administrative culture predominated along the coast, while the penal colony also left a strong mark on literature and public imagination.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系为主，并有法语克里奥尔与原住民语言并存
languageFamily.en: Primarily Indo-European, alongside French creole and Indigenous languages

philosophy.zh: 以法国共和主义、天主教文化遗产与边缘殖民地生存实用主义相结合为特点。
philosophy.en: Characterized by a blend of French republicanism, Catholic cultural heritage, and the practical ethos of a marginal colony.

writingSystem.zh: 使用拉丁字母，官方语言为法语，民间亦使用法属克里奥尔语及原住民语言。
writingSystem.en: Used the Latin alphabet; French was the official language, while French Creole and Indigenous languages were also spoken.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模极小，商业活动集中于卡宴和少数沿海点，以行政消费、有限农业、金矿开采和补给贸易为主。刑罚殖民地体系虽提供劳力和政府支出，但并未形成强劲的自我增长经济。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was extremely small, with commercial activity concentrated in Cayenne and a few coastal points, relying on administrative spending, limited agriculture, gold mining, and supply trade. The penal colony system provided labor and government expenditure but did not create a strong self-sustaining growth economy.

mainIndustries.zh: 行政服务、金矿开采、小规模农业、木材、渔业、监狱供应体系
mainIndustries.en: Administrative services, gold mining, small-scale agriculture, timber, fishing, and penal supply systems

tradeGoods.zh: 黄金、木材、朗姆酒、小宗农产品、进口食品、衣物与行政物资
tradeGoods.en: Gold, timber, rum, minor agricultural produce, imported food, clothing, and administrative supplies

householdWealth.zh: 居民家庭财富普遍有限，少数商人和官员生活较为稳定，而普通克里奥尔居民、劳工和刑罚体系相关人口资产微薄。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was generally limited; a small number of merchants and officials enjoyed stable living standards, while ordinary Creole residents, laborers, and penal-system populations possessed little property.

economicSystem.zh: 依附法国本土财政与行政支出的边缘殖民经济，兼具少量资源开采和供给型贸易特征。
economicSystem.en: A peripheral colonial economy dependent on metropolitan French finance and administrative spending, with limited resource extraction and supply-oriented trade.

treasuryDescription.zh: 库藏有限且依赖法国本土拨付，难以独立承担长期危机或大规模基础设施建设。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was limited and dependent on metropolitan remittances, making it unable to independently sustain prolonged crises or major infrastructure works.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重点在维持刑罚殖民运作、最低限度行政秩序和基本卫生设施，属于高度依赖外部补助的防守型政策。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining the penal colony, minimal administrative order, and basic sanitary facilities, amounting to a defensive strategy heavily dependent on outside subsidy.

name.zh: 殖民地步兵与海军陆战守备队

description.zh: 驻守卡宴、监狱设施和重要行政据点，承担基础防务任务。
description.en: Garrisoned Cayenne, penal facilities, and key administrative posts, providing basic defense.

description.zh: 维持治安、看守流放犯并管理沿海及岛屿刑场。
description.en: Maintained public order, guarded convicts, and administered coastal and island penal sites.

description.zh: 负责小型港防、巡逻艇和沿岸交通警戒。
description.en: Handled minor port defense, patrol craft, and coastal traffic surveillance.

commanderInChief.zh: 法属圭亚那总督

technology.zh: 装备包括法军制式步枪、轻炮、小型巡逻艇和电报通信，但总体规模很小，以守备和监管为主。
technology.en: Equipment included standard French rifles, light artillery, small patrol craft, and telegraph communications, but the force was very small and oriented toward garrison and supervision.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自疾病、逃犯、走私、内陆控制困难以及边境荒僻地区的行政空缺，而非外部正规战争。
threats.en: Main threats came from disease, escaped convicts, smuggling, difficult interior control, and administrative gaps in remote borderlands rather than external conventional war.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约3万，集中在卡宴及少数沿海聚落，整体密度极低。自由居民与刑罚流放系统人口并存，使人口结构不同于一般殖民地。
populationDescription.en: The population was about 30,000, concentrated in Cayenne and a few coastal settlements, with extremely low overall density. The coexistence of free residents and penal-system populations gave it a demographic profile unlike that of most colonies.

socialClasses.zh: 上层主要是法国官员、军警和少数商人；中层为职员、小店主与自由手工业者；下层包括普通劳动者、贫困克里奥尔人、监狱相关人口和偏远地区原住民社群。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum was made up mainly of French officials, military-police personnel, and a few merchants; the middle consisted of clerks, small shopkeepers, and free artisans; the lower ranks included ordinary laborers, poor Creoles, penal populations, and remote Indigenous communities.

allies.zh: 法国本土与法兰西殖民帝国；与邻近荷属、英属地区保持有限边务协作。
allies.en: Metropolitan France and the French colonial empire; maintained limited frontier cooperation with neighboring Dutch and British territories.

enemies.zh: 无正式敌国，主要问题是边界管理、走私与区域孤立。
enemies.en: No formal enemy state; chief issues were frontier management, smuggling, and regional isolation.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策完全由法国制定，重点在维持主权象征、管理边界并将该地作为流放与帝国存在的边缘据点。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was wholly set by France, focusing on preserving symbols of sovereignty, managing borders, and using the colony as a marginal outpost of penal transportation and imperial presence.

era.zh: 第二次工业革命时期的低开发度法国边缘殖民地
era.en: A low-development peripheral French colony during the Second Industrial Revolution

keyInnovations.zh: 电报、蒸汽补给船、热带卫生措施、刑罚殖民管理制度和小规模金矿勘探技术。
keyInnovations.en: Telegraphy, steam supply shipping, tropical sanitary measures, penal-colony administrative systems, and small-scale gold prospecting techniques.

strengths.zh: 法国国家支持提供最低限度稳定，卡宴作为行政中心维持法属主权，金矿资源带来有限补充收入。
strengths.en: Support from the French state provided a minimum of stability, Cayenne sustained French sovereignty as an administrative center, and gold resources supplied limited supplementary income.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀少、经济基础薄弱、热带疾病严重、刑罚殖民污名深重且高度依赖本土财政。
weaknesses.en: It suffered from a sparse population, weak economic foundations, severe tropical disease, the stigma of the penal colony, and heavy dependence on metropolitan finance.

outlook.zh: 短期内将继续作为法国维持主权和流放制度的边缘据点存在，但若无更大移民与投资，难以发展为繁荣殖民地。
outlook.en: In the short term it would continue as a marginal French outpost for sovereignty and penal transportation, but without greater immigration and investment it was unlikely to become a prosperous colony.

description.zh: 法属圭亚那是一个人口稀少、经济薄弱且深受刑罚殖民制度影响的法国热带殖民地。
description.en: French Guiana was a sparsely populated French tropical colony with a weak economy and a social order deeply shaped by the penal colony system.

dynasty.en: British Colonial Administration of the Gambia

governmentForm.zh: 冈比亚在1900年前后为英国属地，由驻巴瑟斯特的总督及殖民官僚体系统治，并通过地方酋长间接管理内地。
governmentForm.en: Around 1900, the Gambia was a British possession governed by a governor in Bathurst through a colonial bureaucracy and indirect rule over inland chiefs.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以曼丁卡、沃洛夫、富拉等族群的农村穆斯林社群为主体，沿河聚落依赖花生种植与地方贸易。巴瑟斯特则形成较小的殖民城市社会，包括欧洲官员、阿库与克里奥尔中间阶层、商人及码头劳工。传统酋长、伊斯兰教士与殖民行政人员共同影响地方秩序。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on rural Muslim communities of Mandinka, Wolof, Fula, and other groups, with riverine settlements dependent on peanut cultivation and local trade. Bathurst hosted a small colonial urban society of European officials, Aku and Creole intermediaries, merchants, and dock laborers. Traditional chiefs, Islamic clerics, and colonial administrators all shaped local order.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由英国总督、殖民文官、少量驻军军官及与殖民政府合作的地方酋长构成。巴瑟斯特的欧洲商号与受教育的非洲精英在经济与行政上也拥有一定影响力。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the British governor, colonial civil servants, a small officer corps, and local chiefs cooperating with the administration. European merchant houses and a small educated African elite in Bathurst also exercised economic and administrative influence.

succession.zh: 总督由英国殖民部任命，并非本地世袭。地方酋长多按族群传统继承，但其地位需得到殖民政府承认。
succession.en: The governor was appointed by the British Colonial Office rather than through local hereditary succession. Local chiefs usually inherited office according to ethnic custom, but their position depended on recognition by the colonial government.

structure.zh: 殖民地行政权集中于总督及其执行委员会，巴瑟斯特为行政核心。英国在沿河据点维持直接统治，对内地则主要依靠酋长、税务代理人与地区专员进行间接管理。由于财政有限，行政体系规模很小。
structure.en: Administrative authority was concentrated in the governor and his executive council, with Bathurst as the central seat of power. Britain maintained direct rule in key river stations while relying on chiefs, tax agents, and district commissioners for inland indirect administration. Because finances were limited, the bureaucratic apparatus remained small.

function.zh: 负责总督政令、文书、殖民联络及总体行政协调。
function.en: Handled gubernatorial orders, records, imperial correspondence, and overall administrative coordination.

function.zh: 征收关税、港口税、贸易税及管理殖民地账目。
function.en: Collected customs, port duties, trade taxes, and managed colonial accounts.

name.zh: 地方事务与酋长管理署

function.zh: 监督内地酋长、处理地方纠纷并执行间接统治。
function.en: Supervised inland chiefs, handled local disputes, and implemented indirect rule.

localAdmin.zh: 巴瑟斯特及少数河口据点由殖民官员直接管理。内地各区依靠酋长治理村社，英国地区官员定期巡视并征收税赋。
localAdmin.en: Bathurst and a few river stations were administered directly by colonial officials. Inland districts were governed through chiefs over village communities, with British district officers making periodic tours and collecting taxes.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系为英国殖民法与地方习惯法并行。涉及婚姻、继承和部分土地事务时，穆斯林法官与传统权威仍发挥作用。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined British colonial law with local customary law. In matters such as marriage, inheritance, and some land issues, Muslim judges and traditional authorities still played important roles.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依靠进出口关税、贸易执照费和若干地方税。内地税收征解能力有限，因此海关收入占比特别高。
taxationSystem.en: Fiscal income relied mainly on import-export customs duties, trade license fees, and limited local taxes. Since inland tax extraction was weak, customs revenue formed an especially large share.

religion.zh: 伊斯兰教
religion.en: Islam

culturalAchievements.zh: 冈比亚文化以伊斯兰教育、口述史传统、吟游诗人音乐和沿河贸易社会的多族群交流见长。巴瑟斯特也出现了受英国教育影响的报刊与教会学校文化。
culturalAchievements.en: Gambian culture was notable for Islamic learning, oral historical traditions, griot music, and the multiethnic exchange of river trade society. Bathurst also saw small-scale newspaper and mission-school culture influenced by British education.

languageFamily.zh: 尼日尔-刚果语系与大西洋语支诸语言为主，辅以阿拉伯语宗教用语和英语行政用语
languageFamily.en: Primarily Niger-Congo and Atlantic languages, supplemented by Arabic in religion and English in administration

philosophy.zh: 以西非伊斯兰传统、苏非教团影响与地方习俗伦理并存
philosophy.en: A blend of West African Islamic tradition, Sufi influence, and local customary ethics

writingSystem.zh: 阿拉伯字母经文书写与拉丁字母殖民行政文书并用
writingSystem.en: Arabic-script religious writing coexisted with Latin-script colonial administration

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模较小，核心依赖花生出口、沿河贸易和港口服务。农业生产多为低资本投入的乡村经营，殖民地财政和商业活动都深受国际花生行情影响。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was small, centered on peanut exports, river trade, and port services. Agricultural production was mostly low-capital rural cultivation, and both public finance and commerce were highly sensitive to world peanut prices.

mainIndustries.zh: 花生种植、河运贸易、港口装卸、渔业、小规模手工业
mainIndustries.en: Peanut cultivation, river transport trade, port handling, fishing, and small-scale handicrafts

tradeGoods.zh: 花生、棕榈制品、兽皮、蜡、米、纺织品、盐、酒类与进口杂货
tradeGoods.en: Peanuts, palm products, hides, wax, rice, textiles, salt, liquor, and imported general goods

householdWealth.zh: 普通乡村家庭财富主要体现为土地使用权、粮食储备、牲畜及花生收成，现金持有量有限。巴瑟斯特商人和中介阶层的货币财富明显高于内地农户。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary rural household wealth was held mainly in land-use rights, grain stocks, livestock, and peanut harvests, with limited cash holdings. Merchants and intermediaries in Bathurst possessed substantially more monetized wealth than inland farmers.

economicSystem.zh: 殖民港口导向型经济，结合乡村商品作物生产与欧洲商号控制的出口贸易网络。
economicSystem.en: A colonial port-oriented economy combining rural cash-crop production with export networks dominated by European merchant houses.

treasuryDescription.zh: 殖民地库藏规模有限，主要用于平抑贸易波动与支付季节性行政开支。遇到军务或公共工程增加时，往往需要帝国支援或临时借拨。
treasuryDescription.en: The colonial treasury was modest in size and used mainly to smooth trade fluctuations and meet seasonal administrative expenses. Military demands or public works often required imperial support or temporary advances.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向收支平衡与低成本统治，优先保障海关征收和治安支出，公共投资相对有限。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored balanced budgets and low-cost rule, prioritizing customs collection and security spending while keeping public investment limited.

description.zh: 由英国军官指挥的驻地步兵，负责巴瑟斯特及河岸据点防务。
description.en: Station infantry under British officers responsible for Bathurst and key river posts.

description.zh: 承担巡逻、税收协助及内地秩序维持任务。
description.en: Handled patrols, assistance in tax collection, and maintenance of inland order.

description.zh: 配合海军巡逻河口与运输线，规模很小。
description.en: A very small naval detachment supporting patrols of the estuary and transport lines.

commanderInChief.zh: 英国总督兼殖民地驻军最高长官
commanderInChief.en: British Governor as supreme colonial authority over the garrison

technology.zh: 装备以栓动步枪、轻炮、轮船运输和电报联络为主，技术水平依赖英国帝国体系补给。
technology.en: Equipment centered on bolt-action rifles, light artillery, steam transport, and telegraph communications, with technology dependent on British imperial supply.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自内地局部抗税、边境摩擦、贸易中断与沿河治安问题，而非大规模对外战争。
threats.en: The main threats were localized tax resistance inland, border friction, trade disruption, and riverine security issues rather than major external war.

populationDescription.zh: 人口大多居住在冈比亚河沿岸乡村地区，族群与语言多样，但伊斯兰信仰在社会生活中占主导。城市人口比例很低，巴瑟斯特是唯一明显的殖民港口城市。
populationDescription.en: Most of the population lived in rural settlements along the Gambia River, with considerable ethnic and linguistic diversity but Islam dominant in social life. Urbanization was very low, and Bathurst was the only clearly prominent colonial port town.

socialClasses.zh: 社会大致可分为殖民官员与欧洲商人、受教育的城镇中间阶层、地方酋长与宗教领袖、自由农民与渔民，以及少量依附劳工。
socialClasses.en: Society was broadly divided into colonial officials and European merchants, an educated urban middle stratum, local chiefs and religious leaders, free farmers and fishermen, and a small dependent laboring population.

allies.zh: 英国本土及西非英属殖民体系；与法国西非在边界事务上保持有限合作。
allies.en: The British metropole and the wider British West African colonial system; limited cooperation with French West Africa on boundary matters.

enemies.zh: 无正式国家级宿敌，潜在对立主要来自地方反殖民势力与殖民竞争余波。
enemies.en: No formal state-level enemy; opposition came mainly from local anti-colonial resistance and the aftereffects of imperial rivalry.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策实际上服从英国帝国利益，重点是维持冈比亚河贸易通道、稳定边界并防止其他欧洲势力渗透。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was effectively subordinated to British imperial interests, focusing on securing the Gambia River trade route, stabilizing borders, and preventing encroachment by other European powers.

era.zh: 工业时代晚期的殖民边缘地区
era.en: A peripheral colonial territory in the late Industrial Age

keyInnovations.zh: 蒸汽船河运、电报、现代海关管理、栓动步枪与港口仓储体系。
keyInnovations.en: Steam river transport, telegraphy, modern customs administration, bolt-action rifles, and port warehousing.

strengths.zh: 沿冈比亚河的交通与出口条件良好，花生商品化程度较高，殖民行政成本相对较低。
strengths.en: It benefited from favorable transport and export conditions along the Gambia River, relatively advanced peanut commercialization, and comparatively low administrative costs.

weaknesses.zh: 财政基础狭窄，经济过度依赖单一作物，基础设施薄弱，内地控制力有限。
weaknesses.en: Its fiscal base was narrow, the economy was overdependent on a single crop, infrastructure was weak, and inland control remained limited.

outlook.zh: 若国际花生贸易保持强劲，殖民地可维持稳定增长；但其对单一出口和帝国保护的依赖使前景较为脆弱。
outlook.en: If international peanut trade stayed strong, the colony could maintain stable growth; however, its dependence on a single export and imperial protection made its prospects fragile.

description.zh: 1900年前后的冈比亚是一个以花生出口和河运贸易为核心、由英国低成本统治的西非小型殖民地。
description.en: Around 1900, the Gambia was a small West African British colony centered on peanut exports and river trade under low-cost imperial rule.

dynasty.zh: 奥匈帝国统治下的波斯尼亚-黑塞哥维那共同管理体制
dynasty.en: The Condominium Administration of Bosnia-Herzegovina under Austria-Hungary

governmentForm.zh: 1900年的波斯尼亚-黑塞哥维那由奥匈帝国财政部直接统辖，属于双元帝国共同管理的占领兼行政地区。
governmentForm.en: In 1900 Bosnia-Herzegovina was administered directly by the Austro-Hungarian Joint Finance Ministry as an occupied and administered territory of the Dual Monarchy.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由穆斯林地主与城镇精英、东正教农民群体、天主教农民与神职阶层，以及逐渐扩大的官僚、军人和商人阶层构成。农村人口占绝大多数，农业关系仍保留明显的旧庄园与佃耕色彩。随着铁路、驻军和官僚体系扩展，城市中产阶层开始增长。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of Muslim landowners and urban notables, Orthodox peasant communities, Catholic peasants and clergy, and a growing stratum of bureaucrats, soldiers, and merchants. The vast majority lived in the countryside, where agrarian relations still retained strong estate and tenancy features. With the spread of railways, garrisons, and bureaucracy, an urban middle class began to expand.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层以奥匈派驻总督、军政官员和受帝国扶持的地方显贵为核心。传统穆斯林贝伊家族、塞尔维亚和克罗地亚社群中的宗教与市政精英也在地方层面具有影响力。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was centered on the Austro-Hungarian governor, military-administrative officials, and local notables supported by the empire. Traditional Muslim beys, along with religious and municipal elites among Serb and Croat communities, also held influence at the local level.

succession.zh: 总督与高级官员由维也纳和布达佩斯协调任命，不存在本地王朝继承。地方名门的社会地位多来自地产、宗教职务与帝国承认。
succession.en: The governor and senior officials were appointed through coordination between Vienna and Budapest, with no local dynastic succession. The status of local notable families derived mainly from landholding, religious office, and imperial recognition.

structure.zh: 行政体制具有明显的军政混合特征，由总督领导，并接受帝国共同财政部监督。省内设有地区行政单位、警察、法院和工程机关，以强化秩序、税收与基础设施建设。尽管保留一定地方宗教和市政机构，但实权掌握在帝国官僚手中。
structure.en: The administrative system had a distinctly mixed military-civil character, led by a governor under the supervision of the Joint Finance Ministry. The province was divided into district administrations with police, courts, and engineering offices to strengthen order, taxation, and infrastructure. Although some local religious and municipal institutions remained, effective power rested in imperial bureaucracy.

function.zh: 统筹省政、治安、地方行政与对帝国中央的联络。
function.en: Coordinated provincial governance, security, local administration, and communication with imperial authorities.

function.zh: 负责土地税、消费税、关税配合及预算管理。
function.en: Managed land taxes, excises, customs coordination, and budget administration.

function.zh: 管理法院体系，并协调穆斯林、东正教与天主教社群的法定地位。
function.en: Administered the courts and coordinated the legal status of Muslim, Orthodox, and Catholic communities.

function.zh: 修建铁路、公路、桥梁及官方建筑，推动帝国整合。
function.en: Built railways, roads, bridges, and official buildings to advance imperial integration.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以州县和市镇为单位，由帝国任命官员主持，并辅以市政机构。乡村层面的旧有社群和宗教领袖仍具社会影响，但行政和警察权由帝国官员掌握。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized through districts and municipalities headed by imperial appointees, supported by municipal bodies. Traditional communal and religious leaders retained social influence in the countryside, but administrative and police power lay with imperial officials.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系融合奥匈成文法、行政法和部分宗教身份法。刑事、财政和行政案件由帝国法院处理，而婚姻及部分家事仍受宗教法规范影响。
legalSystem.en: The legal order combined Austro-Hungarian codified law, administrative law, and elements of confessional personal status law. Criminal, fiscal, and administrative cases went to imperial courts, while marriage and some family matters remained influenced by religious law.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以土地税、消费税、垄断收入和行政收费为主，并受帝国财政标准约束。税制较奥斯曼晚期更系统，但也加重了农村社会的现金负担。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation rested on land taxes, excises, monopoly revenues, and administrative fees, under imperial fiscal standards. The system was more systematic than in the late Ottoman period, but it also increased cash burdens on the rural population.

religion.zh: 伊斯兰教/东正教/天主教混合
religion.en: Islam/Orthodoxy/Catholicism

culturalAchievements.zh: 这一时期的波斯尼亚在建筑、城市规划和教育制度上显现出奥斯曼传统与哈布斯堡现代化的混合面貌。萨拉热窝等城市出现报刊、学校、铁路站区与新式行政建筑，形成独特的边疆近代文化景观。
culturalAchievements.en: At this time Bosnia displayed a mixture of Ottoman traditions and Habsburg modernization in architecture, urban planning, and education. Cities such as Sarajevo developed newspapers, schools, railway precincts, and new administrative buildings, creating a distinctive frontier modern culture.

languageFamily.zh: 南斯拉夫语支，属印欧语系；宗教与行政场合亦使用阿拉伯文、土耳其文和德语遗存
languageFamily.en: South Slavic within the Indo-European family; Arabic-script, Ottoman Turkish, and German also persisted in religious or administrative contexts

philosophy.zh: 奥斯曼遗产、斯拉夫民族意识、天主教与东正教宗教思想并存，并逐渐受到中欧官僚现代性的影响
philosophy.en: A coexistence of Ottoman legacy, Slavic national consciousness, Catholic and Orthodox religious thought, increasingly shaped by Central European bureaucratic modernity

writingSystem.zh: 拉丁字母与西里尔字母并用，行政中亦见德语和匈牙利语
writingSystem.en: Latin and Cyrillic scripts were both used, while German and Hungarian also appeared in administration

unit.zh: 克朗（奥匈货币，当时币值）

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、林业、畜牧与矿产开发为基础，并在奥匈统治下逐步接入中欧市场。铁路和行政建设带动了城市商业，但整体仍属中低收入的帝国边缘地区。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on agriculture, forestry, pastoralism, and mineral extraction, while progressively integrating into Central European markets under Austro-Hungarian rule. Railways and administrative construction stimulated urban commerce, but the province remained a lower-income imperial periphery.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、林业、采矿、金属加工、木材加工、铁路与建筑业
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, forestry, mining, metalworking, timber processing, railways, and construction

tradeGoods.zh: 木材、牲畜、皮革、烟草、谷物、煤、铁矿、盐、纺织品与制成品
tradeGoods.en: Timber, livestock, leather, tobacco, grain, coal, iron ore, salt, textiles, and manufactured goods

householdWealth.zh: 农村家庭财富多表现为土地、牲畜、林地使用权和谷物储备，但债务负担不轻。城市家庭则更依赖工资、商贸收入和不动产，贫富差距明显扩大。
householdWealth.en: Rural household wealth was mainly held in land, livestock, forest-use rights, and grain stocks, though debt burdens were significant. Urban households relied more on wages, trade income, and real estate, with widening social disparities.

economicSystem.zh: 帝国统合下的混合经济，农业占主导，国家通过铁路、税务和工程投资推动有限工业化。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy under imperial integration, dominated by agriculture, with the state promoting limited industrialization through railways, taxation, and public works investment.

treasuryDescription.zh: 库藏在省级财政中属中等规模，可维持行政与工程周转，但重大军事需求仍须依赖帝国中央。财政状况较奥斯曼晚期稳定，但高度受政治与治安局势影响。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was moderate by provincial standards, sufficient for administrative and public works operations, but major military needs still depended on the imperial center. Finances were more stable than in the late Ottoman era, yet highly sensitive to political and security conditions.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调秩序维持、基础设施建设和税收正规化，以把该地区更紧密地纳入奥匈帝国经济体系。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized order, infrastructure construction, and tax regularization in order to bind the province more tightly into the Austro-Hungarian economic system.

description.zh: 驻守萨拉热窝、莫斯塔尔、巴尼亚卢卡等地，负责控制交通线与镇压骚乱。
description.en: Stationed at Sarajevo, Mostar, Banja Luka, and other points to control communications and suppress unrest.

description.zh: 适应山地地形，承担炮兵支援、道路修筑和桥梁工程。
description.en: Adapted to mountainous terrain, providing artillery support and building roads and bridges.

description.zh: 执行治安巡逻、边境监视及反叛乱任务。
description.en: Conducted security patrols, border surveillance, and counterinsurgency duties.

commanderInChief.zh: 奥匈总督兼驻军总司令
commanderInChief.en: Austro-Hungarian Governor and commander of the provincial garrison

technology.zh: 军队装备栓动步枪、山炮、铁路运输、野战电报和较完善的参谋体系，技术水平在巴尔干属较先进。
technology.en: The forces used bolt-action rifles, mountain guns, railway transport, field telegraphy, and a comparatively well-developed staff system, making them relatively advanced within the Balkans.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自塞尔维亚民族主义、秘密社团、宗教族群紧张及巴尔干国际局势恶化。
threats.en: The principal threats came from Serbian nationalism, secret societies, intercommunal tension, and the worsening international situation in the Balkans.

populationDescription.zh: 人口由穆斯林、东正教塞尔维亚人和天主教克罗地亚人等主要群体构成，宗教认同与社会身份高度交织。乡村人口占绝对多数，但萨拉热窝、莫斯塔尔等城市因军政和商业发展而持续扩张。
populationDescription.en: The population was composed mainly of Muslims, Orthodox Serbs, and Catholic Croats, with religion and social identity closely intertwined. The countryside contained the overwhelming majority, but cities such as Sarajevo and Mostar continued to grow through military-administrative and commercial expansion.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括帝国官僚与军官、穆斯林地主、神职人员、城市商人和工匠、小农与佃农，以及日益增多的工人和铁路雇员。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included imperial bureaucrats and officers, Muslim landowners, clergy, urban merchants and artisans, smallholders and tenants, and a growing number of workers and railway employees.

allies.zh: 依托奥匈帝国整体外交，与德国保持同盟关联，并受帝国军政体系保护。
allies.en: It relied on Austro-Hungarian diplomacy as a whole, tied to Germany through alliance, and protected by the imperial military-administrative system.

enemies.zh: 潜在对手主要为激进塞尔维亚民族主义网络及反奥匈政治力量；与奥斯曼帝国的直接冲突已明显减弱。
enemies.en: Its principal adversaries were radical Serbian nationalist networks and anti-Habsburg political forces; direct conflict with the Ottoman Empire had largely diminished.

foreignPolicy.zh: 该地区并无独立外交，其对外方向完全服从奥匈帝国：稳固巴尔干边界、遏制塞尔维亚影响并推进经济整合。
foreignPolicy.en: The territory had no independent foreign policy, and its external orientation fully followed Austria-Hungary: securing Balkan frontiers, containing Serbian influence, and advancing economic integration.

era.zh: 工业时代晚期与帝国基础设施扩张期
era.en: Late Industrial Age during a phase of imperial infrastructure expansion

keyInnovations.zh: 窄轨铁路、山地炮兵体系、现代警察档案制度、电报网络、城市供水和公共建筑工程。
keyInnovations.en: Narrow-gauge railways, mountain artillery systems, modern police record-keeping, telegraph networks, urban waterworks, and public building programs.

strengths.zh: 地处巴尔干要冲，矿林资源较丰富，行政与军事能力强，铁路和工程建设推动了整合。
strengths.en: It occupied a strategic Balkan position, possessed substantial mineral and forest resources, and benefited from strong administrative and military capacity alongside railway-led integration.

weaknesses.zh: 民族宗教矛盾深刻，农村贫困与土地问题突出，政治合法性依赖外来统治。
weaknesses.en: Ethno-religious tensions were deep, rural poverty and agrarian problems were serious, and political legitimacy depended on external imperial rule.

outlook.zh: 短期内在奥匈军政控制下可维持稳定与建设，但巴尔干民族主义持续高涨，长期前景隐含重大危机。
outlook.en: In the short term it could remain stable and continue developing under Austro-Hungarian control, but rising Balkan nationalism made the long-term outlook highly precarious.

description.zh: 1900年的波斯尼亚-黑塞哥维那是一个由奥匈帝国强力整合、兼具奥斯曼遗产与中欧现代化特征的巴尔干边疆行政区。
description.en: In 1900 Bosnia-Herzegovina was a Balkan frontier territory strongly integrated by Austria-Hungary, combining Ottoman legacy with Central European modernization.

dynasty.en: British Colonial Government of Hong Kong

governmentForm.zh: 1900年的香港为英国皇家殖民地，由港督统治，并通过行政局、立法局及华洋分层的市政与警务体系管理。
governmentForm.en: In 1900 Hong Kong was a British Crown Colony ruled by a governor through the Executive Council, Legislative Council, and a layered municipal and policing system divided across European and Chinese society.

socialStructure.zh: 香港社会由英国殖民官员、欧洲商行与金融精英、华人买办与商人、中下层工人和船民组成。华人占人口绝大多数，并在商业、码头、手工业和服务业中居主导地位。种族分层与居住隔离明显，但跨帝国贸易使城市流动性极高。
socialStructure.en: Hong Kong society consisted of British colonial officials, European commercial and financial elites, Chinese compradors and merchants, lower-class workers, and boat people. Chinese residents formed the overwhelming majority and dominated commerce, dock work, handicrafts, and services. Racial stratification and residential segregation were pronounced, but the city was highly mobile due to imperial trade.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层以港督、英籍高级文官、驻港军官和洋行资本家为核心。部分华商领袖与买办通过保良局、东华医院及咨询网络获得有限政治影响力。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was centered on the governor, senior British civil servants, military officers, and merchant capitalists. Some leading Chinese merchants and compradors gained limited political influence through institutions such as the Tung Wah Hospital, Po Leung Kuk, and consultative networks.

succession.zh: 港督由英国政府任命，不存在本地世袭。地方社会影响力则常在华商家族、买办网络与慈善机构中延续。
succession.en: The governor was appointed by the British government, with no local hereditary succession. Social influence within the colony often continued through Chinese merchant families, comprador networks, and charitable institutions.

structure.zh: 殖民地行政高度集中于港督及其领导的行政局，立法局主要为咨询与法规制定机关。政府依赖警队、港口管理、公共工程和卫生部门维持秩序与商业环境。华人社会在制度上代表不足，但通过商会、慈善组织和非正式协商发挥作用。
structure.en: Colonial administration was highly centralized in the governor and the Executive Council, while the Legislative Council mainly served consultative and lawmaking functions. The government relied on the police, port management, public works, and sanitary departments to preserve order and a commercial environment. Chinese society was underrepresented institutionally but exerted influence through chambers of commerce, charities, and informal consultation.

function.zh: 负责总体行政、立法协调、对伦敦与华南事务联络。
function.en: Managed overall administration, legislative coordination, and liaison with London and South China affairs.

name.en: Colonial Treasury, Customs, and Harbour Administration

function.zh: 管理财政预算、港口收费、地税、牌照与商业规制。
function.en: Oversaw budgets, harbour dues, land revenue, licensing, and commercial regulation.

function.zh: 负责城市治安、刑事侦缉、监狱和法院运作。
function.en: Handled urban security, criminal investigation, prisons, and court operations.

function.zh: 建设道路、码头、供水、山顶设施并处理公共卫生问题。
function.en: Built roads, piers, waterworks, and hill infrastructure while addressing public health concerns.

localAdmin.zh: 香港岛、九龙及新界由殖民政府分区管理，新界在1898年租借后逐步建立更正式的乡村治理。乡绅、宗族领袖与警署在地方事务中承担桥梁角色。
localAdmin.en: Hong Kong Island, Kowloon, and the New Territories were managed through colonial districts, with more formal rural administration gradually introduced in the New Territories after the 1898 lease. Village elders, clan leaders, and police stations played intermediary roles in local affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以英国普通法为基础，并辅以殖民条例。涉及华人婚姻、宗族和土地习惯时，政府在特定情况下承认部分华人习惯法与乡例。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on English common law supplemented by colonial ordinances. In matters concerning Chinese marriage, lineage, and land customs, the government sometimes recognized elements of Chinese customary law.

taxationSystem.zh: 香港税制偏向低直接税，主要依靠地租地税、牌照费、酒烟税、印花与港口收费。自由港地位使其避免普遍关税，以促进转口贸易。
taxationSystem.en: Hong Kong's tax regime favored low direct taxation, relying mainly on land revenue, license fees, liquor and tobacco duties, stamps, and harbour charges. Its free-port status avoided general customs tariffs in order to promote entrepôt trade.

religion.zh: 中国民间宗教/基督教
religion.en: Chinese folk religion/Christianity

culturalAchievements.zh: 香港在19世纪末已形成中西交汇的都市文化，包括华文报业、英文学堂、慈善机构、现代医院与繁荣的戏曲娱乐。作为自由港，它也是粤语出版、宗教传播和南中国移民网络的重要节点。
culturalAchievements.en: By the late nineteenth century Hong Kong had developed a Sino-Western urban culture marked by Chinese newspapers, English schools, charitable institutions, modern hospitals, and thriving opera entertainment. As a free port, it was also an important node for Cantonese publishing, religious activity, and South China migrant networks.

languageFamily.zh: 汉藏语系汉语族粤语为主，辅以英语及少量客家、潮州等方言
languageFamily.en: Primarily Cantonese within the Sinitic branch of Sino-Tibetan, alongside English and smaller Hakka and Teochew speech communities

philosophy.zh: 华南儒家伦理、民间信仰与英国殖民法治观并存
philosophy.en: South Chinese Confucian ethics and folk religion coexisted with British colonial ideas of law and governance

writingSystem.zh: 汉字为主，英语以拉丁字母用于政府、法律与国际贸易
writingSystem.en: Chinese characters predominated, while English in Latin script served government, law, and international trade

unit.en: Hong Kong dollars per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 香港经济以转口贸易、航运、金融服务、仓储、船坞和对华商业中介为核心。虽然本地制造业规模仍有限，但港口效率、资本流动和帝国保护使其在东亚商业网络中极具活力。
gdpDescription.en: Hong Kong's economy was centered on entrepôt trade, shipping, finance, warehousing, dockyards, and intermediary commerce with China. Although local manufacturing was still limited, port efficiency, capital mobility, and imperial protection made it highly dynamic within East Asian commercial networks.

mainIndustries.zh: 转口贸易、航运、金融、仓储、船坞修理、建筑、印刷与食品加工
mainIndustries.en: Entrepôt trade, shipping, finance, warehousing, dock repair, construction, printing, and food processing

tradeGoods.zh: 鸦片、纺织品、机器、茶叶、丝绸、瓷器、煤炭、米粮、糖、金银与洋货杂品
tradeGoods.en: Opium, textiles, machinery, tea, silk, porcelain, coal, rice, sugar, bullion, and assorted imported goods

householdWealth.zh: 普通华人家庭财富差异极大，商人和买办可积累大量银元、不动产与商业股份，而码头工人、苦力和船民多仅维持低储蓄。城市地产与贸易信用是财富形成的核心。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied sharply: merchants and compradors could accumulate substantial silver coin, real estate, and commercial shares, while dock workers, coolies, and boat people often survived with minimal savings. Urban property and trade credit were central to wealth formation.

unit.en: Hong Kong dollars per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以自由港和殖民法治为基础的开放型商业经济，深度依附英国帝国航运网络并服务华南市场。
economicSystem.en: An open commercial economy based on free-port policy and colonial rule of law, deeply tied to British imperial shipping networks and serving South China markets.

source.en: Liquor, tobacco, stamp, and excise duties

treasuryDescription.zh: 库藏状况稳健，得益于土地收入和港口收费的持续增长。作为贸易港，财政对商业景气度敏感，但整体偿付能力在东亚殖民地中较强。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was in sound condition, supported by growing land revenues and harbour charges. As a trade port, finances were sensitive to commercial cycles, but overall fiscal capacity was strong by East Asian colonial standards.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调低税率、财政盈余、港口与市政投资，并通过稳健预算维护殖民地信用与商业吸引力。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized low taxation, budget surpluses, and investment in port and municipal infrastructure while maintaining colonial creditworthiness and commercial attractiveness.

name.en: British Army Garrison in Hong Kong

description.zh: 负责香港岛、九龙及新界的防务，控制炮台、兵营和要地。
description.en: Responsible for the defense of Hong Kong Island, Kowloon, and the New Territories, controlling forts, barracks, and key positions.

description.zh: 执行港湾防御、巡逻、海上交通保护和对华南沿海的威慑任务。
description.en: Handled harbor defense, patrols, protection of sea traffic, and deterrence along the South China coast.

name.en: Hong Kong Police and Auxiliary Armed Units

description.zh: 承担城市治安、情报监视、边境巡逻和紧急动员职能。
description.en: Performed urban policing, intelligence surveillance, border patrol, and emergency mobilization functions.

commanderInChief.zh: 港督为殖民地最高民政长官，驻港英军由英国军方将领指挥
commanderInChief.en: The Governor was the supreme civil authority, while the Hong Kong garrison was commanded by British military officers

technology.zh: 拥有近代海岸炮、蒸汽军舰、栓动步枪、军港设施、电报与较完善的港务通信网络，在远东属先进水平。
technology.en: It possessed modern coastal artillery, steam warships, bolt-action rifles, naval facilities, telegraphy, and a well-developed harbor communications network, advanced by Far Eastern standards.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自华南政局动荡、海盗与走私、传染病引发的社会危机，以及列强在华竞争背景下的战略压力。
threats.en: Key threats came from instability in South China, piracy and smuggling, socially disruptive epidemics, and strategic pressure from great-power rivalry in China.

populationDescription.zh: 人口以华人为绝对多数，包括广东移民、商人、工人、船民与新界乡民；欧洲人与印度人等少数群体多集中于军政、商业和服务岗位。人口流动频繁，受中国内地动乱和贸易周期影响显著。
populationDescription.en: The population was overwhelmingly Chinese, including Guangdong migrants, merchants, workers, boat people, and New Territories villagers; Europeans, Indians, and other minorities were concentrated in military, administrative, commercial, and service roles. Population movement was frequent and strongly affected by unrest in China and trade cycles.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括英籍官员与资本家、欧亚与华人买办精英、中小商人、文员与教师、工匠和苦力、船民及乡村宗族农民。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included British officials and capitalists, Eurasian and Chinese comprador elites, small and medium merchants, clerks and teachers, artisans and laborers, boat people, and rural lineage farmers.

allies.zh: 依托大英帝国全球海军与外交体系，并与条约口岸网络中的英商和友好列强保持合作。
allies.en: It depended on the global naval and diplomatic system of the British Empire and cooperated with British merchants and friendly powers across the treaty-port network.

enemies.zh: 无固定单一宿敌，但潜在对手包括在华竞争的列强势力、海盗集团及反殖民秘密组织。
enemies.en: It had no single permanent enemy, but potential adversaries included rival great powers in China, pirate groups, and anti-colonial secret societies.

foreignPolicy.zh: 香港本身无独立外交，其对外定位服从英国在华政策：维护通商自由、保护侨民和航运，并扩大在华南的战略立足点。
foreignPolicy.en: Hong Kong had no independent foreign policy; its external role followed British policy in China: preserving commercial freedom, protecting subjects and shipping, and strengthening a strategic foothold in South China.

era.zh: 工业时代晚期的东亚殖民自由港
era.en: A late Industrial Age colonial free port in East Asia

keyInnovations.zh: 蒸汽航运、海底电报、现代船坞、煤炭补给站、山顶缆车、城市供水与近代公共卫生制度。
keyInnovations.en: Steam shipping, submarine telegraph links, modern dockyards, coaling stations, the Peak Tram, urban water supply, and modern public health administration.

strengths.zh: 港口优良、法制与金融环境稳定、转口贸易发达、背靠英国海军与华南腹地市场。
strengths.en: Its strengths were an excellent harbor, stable legal and financial institutions, flourishing entrepôt trade, and support from the Royal Navy combined with access to the South China hinterland.

weaknesses.zh: 粮食与水源对外依赖较强，社会分层尖锐，疫病风险高，本地政治代表性不足。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included strong dependence on external food and water supplies, sharp social stratification, high epidemic risk, and limited local political representation.

outlook.zh: 只要英国在华海权和通商优势延续，香港前景仍将兴旺；但其命运高度系于中国局势与帝国竞争。
outlook.en: So long as British naval power and commercial advantage in China endured, Hong Kong's prospects remained prosperous; yet its fate was closely tied to conditions in China and imperial rivalry.

description.zh: 1900年的香港是一个以自由港贸易、航运金融和英帝国海军保护为基础、快速繁荣的东亚殖民都会。
description.en: In 1900 Hong Kong was a rapidly flourishing East Asian colonial city built on free-port trade, shipping finance, and British naval protection.

governmentForm.zh: 奥兰治自由邦是由布尔人选举产生总统并由人民议会监督的共和国，1900年时在英布战争中维持战时体制。
governmentForm.en: The Orange Free State was a Boer republic with an elected president and a volksraad legislature, operating under wartime conditions in 1900 during the Anglo-Boer War.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以布尔农民家庭、少数城镇商人和官员为核心，乡村白人地主在政治上占主导地位。非洲黑人居民人数众多，主要从事农业劳作、放牧和低薪雇佣劳动，但在政治权利上受到严重限制。战争使地方社群高度军事化，公民身份与骑兵服役密切相关。
socialStructure.en: Society centered on Boer farming families, with a smaller class of town merchants and officials, while rural white landholders dominated politics. The African black population was numerous and worked mainly in agriculture, herding, and low-wage labor, but had sharply restricted political rights. War made local society highly militarized, and citizenship was closely tied to mounted service.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要由布尔裔地主、议会议员、地方司令官和归正会精英构成。总统马蒂努斯·斯泰因以宪政合法性和反英抗战立场获得广泛支持。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted mainly of Boer landowners, legislators, local commandants, and Dutch Reformed church elites. President Martinus Steyn drew broad support from his constitutional legitimacy and anti-British wartime stance.

succession.zh: 总统由选举产生而非世袭，任期制体现共和国传统。战时若总统失能，通常由行政会议与议会安排代理与继任程序。
succession.en: The presidency was elective rather than hereditary, reflecting republican tradition. In wartime, if the president became incapacitated, the executive council and legislature would arrange acting authority and succession.

structure.zh: 中央政府由总统、行政会议和人民议会组成，法律上保持共和国框架，但在战争中大量权力转向军政协调。地方上依靠区政委员和突击队司令体系维持征兵、补给和治安。
structure.en: The central government consisted of the president, an executive council, and the volksraad, preserving the republican framework in law, though wartime shifted much authority toward military-civil coordination. Locally, landdrosts and commando command structures handled mobilization, supply, and order.

function.zh: 统筹行政决策、外交联络与战时总动员。
function.en: Coordinated executive decisions, foreign contacts, and wartime mobilization.

function.zh: 负责税收、土地登记、国库和军需拨款。
function.en: Managed taxation, land registration, the treasury, and military appropriations.

function.zh: 负责法院、警务、地方行政和民事记录。
function.en: Oversaw courts, policing, local administration, and civil records.

function.zh: 组织突击队、军马征集、弹药分配与前线通信。
function.en: Organized commandos, horse requisitioning, ammunition distribution, and front communications.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以区为单位，由地方行政官、治安官和社区长老共同运作。战时地方政府常与突击队指挥链合并执行命令。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized by district, with landdrosts, magistrates, and community elders sharing authority. In wartime, local government often merged in practice with commando chains of command.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以罗马-荷兰法为基础，并受本地成文法与共和传统影响。战时特别法令扩大了征用、拘押和军事审判权。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on Roman-Dutch law, shaped by local statutes and republican custom. Wartime decrees expanded powers of requisition, detention, and military justice.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要来自关税、财产税、执照费和铁路收入。战争期间，国家更多依赖非常征收、借款和物资征用。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied mainly on customs duties, property taxes, license fees, and railway income. During the war, the state depended increasingly on extraordinary levies, borrowing, and requisitioning.

religion.zh: 荷兰归正教
religion.en: Dutch Reformed

culturalAchievements.zh: 该国文化成就主要体现在归正会教育、地方报刊和布尔战争时期的政治演说与回忆录传统。民间赞美诗、骑马射击文化和家族圣经阅读构成其社会文化核心。
culturalAchievements.en: Its cultural achievements lay chiefly in Dutch Reformed schooling, local newspapers, and the wartime tradition of political speeches and memoirs. Psalm singing, mounted marksmanship culture, and family Bible reading formed the core of social life.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系日耳曼语族；主要语言为荷兰语及早期南非荷兰语。
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Germanic; main languages were Dutch and early Afrikaans.

philosophy.zh: 以加尔文主义、布尔共和主义和边疆农民的自治观念为核心。
philosophy.en: Rooted in Calvinism, Boer republicanism, and frontier ideals of local autonomy.

writingSystem.zh: 使用拉丁字母，行政和出版语言主要为荷兰语，也逐渐出现南非荷兰语口语影响。
writingSystem.en: Used the Latin alphabet, with Dutch as the main administrative and print language, increasingly influenced by spoken Afrikaans.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以畜牧业、谷物种植和区域贸易为主，工业基础薄弱。英布战争严重扰乱了交通、征税和农产品外运，使1900年的总产出明显低于和平时期。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on pastoralism, grain farming, and regional trade, with only a weak industrial base. The Anglo-Boer War severely disrupted transport, taxation, and agricultural exports, leaving 1900 output well below peacetime levels.

mainIndustries.zh: 牧羊与养牛、小麦种植、运输、皮革加工、基础贸易
mainIndustries.en: Sheep and cattle raising, wheat farming, transport, leather processing, basic trade

tradeGoods.zh: 羊毛、牲畜、皮革、谷物、马匹、进口枪械与工业制成品
tradeGoods.en: Wool, livestock, hides, grain, horses, imported firearms, and manufactured goods

householdWealth.zh: 白人农户财富主要体现在土地、牛羊、马匹和车辆上，流动现金有限。黑人家庭财富多以牲畜、工具和社区网络体现，受战争破坏更为严重。
householdWealth.en: White household wealth was concentrated in land, cattle, sheep, horses, and wagons, with limited cash reserves. Black household wealth was more often held in livestock, tools, and community networks, and was more severely damaged by war.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业私有制和边疆市场交换为主的共和小农经济，战时带有强烈征用与配给色彩。
economicSystem.en: A republican smallholder economy based on private agriculture and frontier market exchange, heavily shaped by requisitioning and rationing in wartime.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模不大，且战时现金储备迅速消耗。可动用资源更多来自地方征用、票据发行和盟国支援期望。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was small, and wartime rapidly depleted cash reserves. Usable resources came increasingly from local requisitions, note issuance, and hoped-for support from allies.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持战时动员为先，接受赤字并依赖借款、紧急征收和物资征用。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized sustaining wartime mobilization, accepting deficits while relying on borrowing, emergency levies, and requisitioning.

description.zh: 以地方公民骑兵为主，机动性高，擅长远程射击和游击战。
description.en: Predominantly citizen-mounted forces with high mobility, skilled in long-range shooting and guerrilla tactics.

description.zh: 装备少量现代野战炮和部分欧洲输入火炮，用于阵地支援。
description.en: Equipped with a limited number of modern field guns and imported European artillery for positional support.

description.zh: 负责补给、护卫、通信和城镇防务。
description.en: Handled supply, escort, communications, and town defense.

commanderInChief.zh: 总统马蒂努斯·斯泰因（政治领袖）；作战由各区司令统筹
commanderInChief.en: President Martinus Steyn (political leader); operations coordinated by district commandants

technology.zh: 武器以毛瑟步枪、无烟火药和现代野战炮为主，通信依赖电报、信使和铁路节点。缺乏重工业与稳定补给，长期消耗战能力有限。
technology.en: Weapons centered on Mauser rifles, smokeless powder, and modern field artillery, while communications relied on telegraph, couriers, and rail nodes. Lacking heavy industry and stable supply, the state had limited endurance in prolonged war.

threats.zh: 主要威胁是大英帝国正规军的压倒性兵力、铁路控制和焦土政策；其次是补给短缺与地方社会被持续削弱。
threats.en: The main threat was the overwhelming manpower, rail control, and scorched-earth strategy of the British Empire; secondary dangers were supply shortages and the steady erosion of local society.

populationDescription.zh: 人口由布尔白人、非洲黑人社群及少量英裔与其他欧洲移民组成。大多数居民分散在农场和小型聚落，城市化水平很低。
populationDescription.en: The population included Boer whites, African black communities, and a small number of British and other European settlers. Most people lived on farms and in small settlements, and urbanization remained low.

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括布尔地主农民、城镇商人与专业人士、贫困白人、黑人佃农与劳工，以及少量行政官员和传教士。
socialClasses.en: Main social classes included Boer landowning farmers, town merchants and professionals, poor whites, black tenants and laborers, and a small number of officials and missionaries.

allies.zh: 主要盟友为德兰士瓦共和国，并期待欧洲列强舆论或间接同情。
allies.en: Its main ally was the Transvaal Republic, while it hoped for European sympathy or indirect support.

enemies.zh: 主要敌人为大英帝国及其南非殖民军。
enemies.en: Its principal enemy was the British Empire and its South African colonial forces.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以维护独立、联合布尔共和国抗英并争取国际承认为核心，但实际外交空间极为有限。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving independence, coordinating Boer resistance against Britain, and seeking international recognition, though practical diplomatic room was very limited.

era.zh: 晚期工业时代与帝国主义战争时期
era.en: Late Industrial Age and era of imperial warfare

keyInnovations.zh: 无烟火药步枪、野战炮、铁路调度、电报通信和战地侦察。
keyInnovations.en: Smokeless-powder rifles, field artillery, rail logistics, telegraph communications, and battlefield scouting.

strengths.zh: 骑兵机动性强、射手素质高、地方认同凝聚、熟悉地形。
strengths.en: Strong mounted mobility, skilled marksmen, cohesive local identity, and intimate knowledge of terrain.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少、财政狭窄、工业薄弱、外交孤立，难以承受长期总体战。
weaknesses.en: Small population, narrow finances, weak industry, and diplomatic isolation made prolonged total war difficult.

outlook.zh: 若无外部干预，1900年后其独立前景趋于恶化，最终很可能被英国军事与行政力量压倒。
outlook.en: Without outside intervention, its prospects for independence worsened after 1900, and it was likely to be overwhelmed by British military and administrative power.

description.zh: 奥兰治自由邦是一个以布尔农民共和国传统为基础、在1900年英布战争中奋力抵抗英国扩张的南部非洲小共和国。
description.en: The Orange Free State was a small southern African Boer republic rooted in agrarian republican traditions and fighting British expansion in 1900.

governmentForm.zh: 德兰士瓦共和国是由总统、执行委员会与人民议会共同治理的布尔共和国，1900年处于全面战争状态。
governmentForm.en: The Transvaal Republic was a Boer republic governed by a president, executive council, and volksraad, and in 1900 it was under conditions of full-scale war.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由布尔农民地主、矿业资本、城市商人和数量庞大的非洲黑人劳工共同构成。威特沃特斯兰德金矿区形成了更复杂的城市社会，而乡村地区仍以白人农牧民和黑人劳工关系为基础。外来移民人口增加也带来了政治冲突，尤其围绕选举权与矿业利益。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of Boer agrarian landholders, mining capital, urban merchants, and a large African black labor force. The Witwatersrand goldfields created a more complex urban society, while rural areas still rested on relations between white pastoral farmers and black laborers. Growing immigrant populations also generated political conflict, especially over franchise rights and mining interests.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由总统保罗·克留格尔、保守派布尔政治家、地方司令官以及与国家合作的矿业和金融人物组成。乡村布尔选民在国家政治中仍占核心地位。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was made up of President Paul Kruger, conservative Boer politicians, local commandants, and mining and financial figures willing to work with the state. Rural Boer voters still formed the political core of the republic.

succession.zh: 总统由选举产生，继任遵循共和宪制而非世袭。若总统离任或流亡，行政机关和议会可指定代理并组织新的政治安排。
succession.en: The president was elected, and succession followed republican constitutional rules rather than heredity. If the president left office or went into exile, executive authorities and the legislature could appoint acting leadership and arrange new political procedures.

structure.zh: 中央政府包括总统、执行委员会和第一、第二人民议会，兼具边疆共和国与近代矿业国家特征。1900年战争使行政机构与军事指挥高度融合，首都虽设于比勒陀利亚，但战时政府运作相当流动。
structure.en: The central government included the president, executive council, and First and Second Volksraads, combining features of a frontier republic and a modern mining state. War in 1900 tightly fused administration and military command, and although Pretoria remained the capital, wartime governance became highly mobile.

function.zh: 负责国家决策、外交、战时协调和高级任命。
function.en: Handled state decisions, diplomacy, wartime coordination, and senior appointments.

function.zh: 管理税收、矿业特许、关税、铁路收入和国债事务。
function.en: Managed taxation, mining concessions, customs, railway revenue, and public debt.

function.zh: 负责法院、警务、地方行政、户籍和移民监管。
function.en: Oversaw courts, policing, local administration, civil registration, and immigration control.

function.zh: 组织突击队、军械采购、炮兵部署与后勤运输。
function.en: Organized commandos, arms procurement, artillery deployment, and logistical transport.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托各区行政官、治安法官与社区网络，在矿区和农区差异明显。战时各区司令对地方动员和运输拥有更大实际权力。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on district officials, magistrates, and community networks, with clear differences between mining and farming areas. In wartime, district commandants gained greater practical authority over mobilization and transport.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以罗马-荷兰法为基础，并叠加矿业、移民和治安方面的特别法规。对外来移民的政治限制与战时戒严使司法带有强烈防卫色彩。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on Roman-Dutch principles, supplemented by special regulations on mining, immigration, and public order. Political restrictions on foreign immigrants and wartime emergency measures gave justice a strongly defensive character.

taxationSystem.zh: 国家收入高度依赖金矿相关税费、关税、铁路和执照费。战争爆发后，矿业税基受损，政府转向借款、征用和非常财政手段。
taxationSystem.en: State revenue depended heavily on gold-mining taxes and fees, customs, railways, and licenses. After war began, the mining tax base was damaged, forcing the government toward loans, requisitions, and extraordinary fiscal measures.

religion.zh: 荷兰归正教
religion.en: Dutch Reformed

culturalAchievements.zh: 德兰士瓦的文化表现结合了布尔共和国政治传统与金矿城市的报刊、教育和宗教活动。战争期间形成大量战地书信、回忆录和民族叙事，影响后来的南非白人政治文化。
culturalAchievements.en: Transvaal's cultural life combined Boer republican traditions with the newspapers, education, and religious activity of the gold-mining towns. The war generated many letters, memoirs, and national narratives that later shaped South African white political culture.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系日耳曼语族；主要语言为荷兰语、早期南非荷兰语，另有英语和多种班图语言。
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Germanic; principal languages were Dutch and early Afrikaans, alongside English and several Bantu languages.

philosophy.zh: 以加尔文主义、布尔民族主义、边疆独立观和反帝国主义情绪为主。
philosophy.en: Guided by Calvinism, Boer nationalism, frontier independence, and anti-imperial sentiment.

writingSystem.zh: 采用拉丁字母，官方与法律语言主要为荷兰语，民间日常语言中南非荷兰语成分上升。
writingSystem.en: Used the Latin alphabet, with Dutch as the principal official and legal language, while Afrikaans elements were increasingly present in everyday speech.

gdpDescription.zh: 德兰士瓦是南部非洲最具活力的矿业经济体之一，金矿尤其重要。尽管1900年战争重创生产和运输，其总体经济规模仍明显大于其他布尔共和国。
gdpDescription.en: Transvaal was one of the most dynamic mining economies in southern Africa, with gold especially important. Although the war in 1900 badly damaged production and transport, its overall economic scale remained much larger than that of the other Boer republics.

mainIndustries.zh: 金矿开采、运输铁路、畜牧业、农牧产品贸易、基础加工业
mainIndustries.en: Gold mining, rail transport, pastoralism, agricultural trade, and basic processing industries

tradeGoods.zh: 黄金、煤、牲畜、皮革、羊毛、进口机械、炸药、铁路设备
tradeGoods.en: Gold, coal, livestock, hides, wool, imported machinery, explosives, and railway equipment

householdWealth.zh: 白人乡村家庭财富以土地、牲畜和马车为主，城市富裕阶层则可持有矿业股份和商业资产。非洲工人家庭财富水平显著较低，且高度受工资波动与强制劳工制度影响。
householdWealth.en: White rural household wealth lay mainly in land, livestock, and wagons, while urban elites could hold mining shares and commercial assets. African worker households possessed much lower wealth levels and were highly exposed to wage fluctuations and coercive labor arrangements.

economicSystem.zh: 属于农业边疆与资本密集型矿业并存的混合经济，国家对矿业特许、铁路和关税有较强干预。
economicSystem.en: It was a mixed economy combining an agrarian frontier with capital-intensive mining, with strong state intervention in mining concessions, railways, and tariffs.

treasuryDescription.zh: 虽有较大的战前矿业税基，但持续战争、首都威胁和运输中断使国库迅速紧张。黄金资源提供潜在信用，但难以在战时完全转化为稳定现金流。
