treasuryDescription.en: Despite a large prewar mining tax base, prolonged conflict, the threat to the capital, and transport disruptions quickly strained the treasury. Gold resources offered potential credit, but they could not be fully converted into stable wartime cash flow.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持战争与国家信用，依赖矿业收入余脉、借款、纸币与征用并行。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed above all to sustain the war and preserve state credit, relying on residual mining income, borrowing, paper issues, and requisitioning.

description.zh: 共和国军力核心，按地区动员，善于快速机动、伏击和远距离精准射击。
description.en: The core of republican military power, mobilized by district and skilled in rapid movement, ambush, and long-range accurate fire.

description.zh: 装备克虏伯和法制火炮，是布尔军中训练最正规、技术含量最高的兵种之一。
description.en: Equipped with Krupp and French guns, it was one of the best trained and most technically advanced Boer arms.

description.zh: 承担要地守备、押运、城镇维持和情报侦察。
description.en: Handled key-point defense, escorts, town security, and intelligence work.

commanderInChief.zh: 总统保罗·克留格尔（政治领袖）；总司令由布尔将领体系负责
commanderInChief.en: President Paul Kruger (political leader); military command exercised by the Boer general staff and senior commanders

technology.zh: 军队装备现代毛瑟步枪、无烟火药、速射野战炮和电报通信，并能利用铁路进行调动。其弱点在于工业制造能力不足、炮弹补充困难和统一参谋体制有限。
technology.en: The army fielded modern Mauser rifles, smokeless powder, quick-firing field artillery, and telegraph communications, and could use railways for movement. Its weaknesses were inadequate industrial production, difficulty replacing ammunition, and a limited unified staff system.

threats.zh: 主要威胁为英军增援、铁路封锁、矿区失守和焦土政策；内部则有外来人口忠诚度不一、补给线拉长和财政枯竭问题。
threats.en: Principal threats were British reinforcements, rail blockades, loss of mining zones, and scorched-earth policies; internally, uneven loyalty among immigrant populations, overstretched supply lines, and fiscal exhaustion also posed dangers.

populationDescription.zh: 人口结构复杂，包含布尔农民、约翰内斯堡矿区外来白人、非洲黑人劳工与多种地方族群。城市与矿区增长迅速，使德兰士瓦较其他布尔国家更具近代化与分层化特征。
populationDescription.en: The population was complex, including Boer farmers, immigrant whites in the Johannesburg mining zone, African black laborers, and multiple local peoples. Rapid urban and mining growth made Transvaal more modernized and socially stratified than other Boer states.

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括布尔地主农民、矿业资本家、城镇中产与技工、贫困白人、非洲矿工与农牧劳工，以及少量国家官员和宗教人士。
socialClasses.en: Main social classes included Boer landowning farmers, mining capitalists, an urban middle class and artisans, poor whites, African miners and farm laborers, and a smaller group of state officials and clergy.

allies.zh: 主要盟友是奥兰治自由邦，并试图争取德意志、荷兰及其他欧洲舆论支持。
allies.en: Its main ally was the Orange Free State, and it sought support from German, Dutch, and other European opinion.

enemies.zh: 主要敌人为大英帝国及其在南部非洲的殖民当局。
enemies.en: Its principal enemy was the British Empire and its colonial authorities in southern Africa.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交方针是保卫主权、限制英国政治渗透并维护对金矿和内陆交通的控制，但在战争中转为争取国际同情与谈判空间。
foreignPolicy.en: Its foreign policy aimed to defend sovereignty, limit British political penetration, and preserve control over the goldfields and inland communications, but in wartime shifted toward seeking international sympathy and room for negotiation.

era.zh: 高帝国主义时代的矿业工业化与现代步枪战争时期
era.en: High imperial age of mining industrialization and modern rifle warfare

keyInnovations.zh: 深井金矿开采、炸药与压缩空气设备、铁路、电报、无烟火药步枪和速射炮。
keyInnovations.en: Deep-level gold mining, explosives and compressed-air equipment, railways, telegraphy, smokeless-powder rifles, and quick-firing artillery.

strengths.zh: 拥有金矿资源、较大人口与财政基础、优秀骑兵和较强炮兵技术。
strengths.en: It possessed gold resources, a larger population and fiscal base, excellent mounted forces, and relatively strong artillery expertise.

weaknesses.zh: 外交孤立、工业不足、政治上难整合外来人口，且无法长期与帝国级对手比拼消耗。
weaknesses.en: It was diplomatically isolated, industrially insufficient, politically unable to fully integrate immigrant populations, and unable to match an imperial opponent in prolonged attrition.

outlook.zh: 1900年虽仍具顽强抵抗能力，但随着英军兵力与后勤优势扩大，其独立前景趋向黯淡。
outlook.en: In 1900 it still retained strong resistance capacity, but as British manpower and logistical superiority expanded, its prospects for independence grew dim.

description.zh: 德兰士瓦共和国是以布尔政治传统和金矿经济为基础、在1900年英布战争中同英国激烈对抗的南非内陆共和国。
description.en: The Transvaal Republic was an inland South African republic built on Boer political traditions and a gold-mining economy, locked in fierce conflict with Britain in 1900.

governmentForm.zh: 纳塔尔是大英帝国下属的自治程度有限殖民地，由总督代表英国王室统治并与殖民地议会共同管理。
governmentForm.en: Natal was a British imperial colony with limited self-government, ruled by a governor representing the Crown in conjunction with a colonial legislature.

socialStructure.zh: 社会结构高度分层，由英裔殖民者、布尔居民、数量庞大的祖鲁人及其他非洲社群、印度契约劳工与商人组成。沿海城市和糖业种植区较为商业化，而内陆农村仍由殖民边疆秩序和非洲传统社会并存。种族法律与劳动制度深刻影响政治参与、土地占有和收入分配。
socialStructure.en: Society was highly stratified, consisting of British settlers, Boer inhabitants, large Zulu and other African communities, and Indian indentured laborers and merchants. Coastal towns and sugar districts were relatively commercialized, while inland rural areas combined colonial frontier order with enduring African social structures. Racial laws and labor systems strongly shaped political participation, landholding, and income distribution.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由英国总督、殖民地官僚、英裔地主与商人、糖业资本家及与殖民政权合作的地方精英构成。殖民立法机构主要代表白人定居者利益。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the British governor, colonial bureaucrats, British landowners and merchants, sugar planters, and local elites cooperating with the colonial regime. The colonial legislature primarily represented white settler interests.

succession.zh: 总督由英国政府任命，不实行本地世袭或选举继承。殖民地行政延续依靠帝国任命制度和责任内阁安排。
succession.en: The governor was appointed by the British government, with neither local hereditary nor electoral succession. Administrative continuity depended on imperial appointment and responsible-government arrangements.

structure.zh: 纳塔尔政府由总督、行政委员会和殖民地议会组成，并接受伦敦殖民部的最终监督。1900年英布战争期间，军事与民政协调明显加强，德班与彼得马里茨堡成为关键后方节点。
structure.en: Natal's government consisted of the governor, an executive council, and a colonial legislature, under the ultimate supervision of the Colonial Office in London. During the Anglo-Boer War in 1900, coordination between military and civil administration intensified, with Durban and Pietermaritzburg serving as critical rear-area centers.

function.zh: 统筹殖民行政、对伦敦报告、战时协调与高级任命。
function.en: Coordinated colonial administration, reporting to London, wartime management, and senior appointments.

function.zh: 负责预算、关税、港口收费、铁路财务与公共债务。
function.en: Managed budgets, customs duties, port charges, railway finances, and public debt.

function.zh: 主管法院、警务、监狱以及对祖鲁和其他非洲社群的行政管理。
function.en: Oversaw courts, policing, prisons, and administrative control over Zulu and other African communities.

function.zh: 管理道路、铁路、港口、电报和军运基础设施。
function.en: Managed roads, railways, ports, telegraphs, and infrastructure for military transport.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由殖民区官员、市政机构和原住民事务系统共同构成。对非洲社群常通过酋长、行政官和特别法规实行间接统治与直接监督并用。
localAdmin.en: Local administration combined district officials, municipal bodies, and the apparatus of native affairs. African communities were often governed through a mix of indirect rule via chiefs and direct supervision by officials under special regulations.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以英国普通法、殖民条例和针对原住民与契约劳工的特别法规并行为特征。不同族群在司法适用和权利保障上并不平等。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined British common law, colonial ordinances, and special regulations for Africans and indentured laborers. Different communities did not enjoy equal legal treatment or rights protection.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自海关、铁路、执照费、土地与人头税。殖民税制兼具贸易财政和劳动力控制功能，尤其对非洲与印度人口影响较大。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from customs, railways, licenses, land taxes, and poll taxes. The colonial tax system served both fiscal and labor-control purposes, especially affecting African and Indian populations.

religion.zh: 基督教
religion.en: Christianity

culturalAchievements.zh: 纳塔尔的文化成就体现在港口城市报刊、传教学校、殖民公共建筑以及英语—祖鲁语接触地带的教育与翻译工作。德班逐渐发展为多族群商业和印刷中心。
culturalAchievements.en: Natal's cultural achievements appeared in port-city newspapers, mission schools, colonial public architecture, and educational and translation work in the English-Zulu contact zone. Durban was gradually developing into a multiethnic commercial and printing center.

languageFamily.zh: 以印欧语系日耳曼语族的英语、印欧语系印度-伊朗语族语言，以及尼日尔-刚果语系班图语支的祖鲁语并存。
languageFamily.en: It combined English from the Indo-European Germanic family, Indo-Iranian languages, and Zulu from the Niger-Congo Bantu branch.

philosophy.zh: 官方文化以英国帝国主义、基督教传教、殖民自由主义与种族等级观并存为特征。
philosophy.en: Official culture was marked by British imperialism, Christian missionary influence, colonial liberalism, and racial hierarchy.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用拉丁字母；英语为行政语言，祖鲁语和印地语等语言在社会与宗教生活中广泛存在。
writingSystem.en: The Latin alphabet predominated; English was the administrative language, while Zulu, Hindi, and other languages were widely present in social and religious life.

gdpDescription.zh: 纳塔尔经济以港口贸易、糖业、铁路运输和殖民农业为支柱，并因英布战争而成为重要补给基地。德班港和通往内陆的铁路使其在南部非洲帝国网络中地位突出。
gdpDescription.en: Natal's economy rested on port trade, sugar production, rail transport, and colonial agriculture, and the Anglo-Boer War turned it into a major supply base. The port of Durban and railways to the interior gave it a prominent place in the southern African imperial network.

mainIndustries.zh: 糖业种植与加工、港口贸易、铁路运输、畜牧业、煤炭与基础制造
mainIndustries.en: Sugar planting and processing, port commerce, railway transport, livestock raising, coal, and basic manufacturing

tradeGoods.zh: 糖、煤、羊毛、皮革、玉米、进口纺织品、机械、军需物资
tradeGoods.en: Sugar, coal, wool, hides, maize, imported textiles, machinery, and military supplies

householdWealth.zh: 白人定居者财富集中于土地、糖厂、商铺和运输资产。非洲家庭财富更多体现为土地使用、牲畜和亲族网络，而印度社群中则存在从契约劳工到小商人的显著差异。
householdWealth.en: White settler wealth was concentrated in land, sugar mills, shops, and transport assets. African household wealth was more often rooted in land use, livestock, and kin networks, while Indian communities ranged widely from indentured laborers to small traders.

economicSystem.zh: 属于依附大英帝国市场的殖民资本主义经济，以港口、铁路和种植园劳动体系为核心。
economicSystem.en: It was a colonial capitalist economy tied to imperial British markets, centered on ports, railways, and plantation labor systems.

source.zh: 土地税、人头税与执照费
source.en: Land taxes, poll taxes, and licenses

treasuryDescription.zh: 纳塔尔财政较布尔共和国更稳定，因港口和铁路收入可持续且可获帝国信用支持。尽管战争推高支出，其国库仍具较强周转能力。
treasuryDescription.en: Natal's finances were more stable than those of the Boer republics because port and railway revenues were more sustainable and backed by imperial credit. Although war raised spending, the treasury retained comparatively strong liquidity.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调贸易税收、基础设施投资和在战时依赖帝国信用进行扩张性支出。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized trade taxation, infrastructure investment, and wartime expansionary spending supported by imperial credit.

description.zh: 包括英国驻军、殖民地志愿兵和重要据点守军，用于防御铁路、城镇和补给线。
description.en: Included British regulars, colonial volunteers, and key garrison troops for defending railways, towns, and supply lines.

name.zh: 纳塔尔骑兵与志愿骑警
name.en: Natal Mounted Troops and Volunteer Mounted Police

description.zh: 负责侦察、边境巡逻、快速支援和追击布尔突击队。
description.en: Handled reconnaissance, frontier patrol, rapid support, and pursuit of Boer commandos.

description.zh: 承担火炮支援、桥梁修复、电报维护和铁路工程保障。
description.en: Provided artillery support, bridge repair, telegraph maintenance, and railway engineering support.

commanderInChief.zh: 英国总督与英军南非战区司令体系
commanderInChief.en: The British Governor together with the British South African theater command

technology.zh: 军力依托英国帝国技术体系，拥有现代步枪、速射炮、装甲列车、电报和成熟铁路后勤。与布尔军相比，其优势在持续补给、工兵与海运支持。
technology.en: Military strength drew on the British imperial technological system, with modern rifles, quick-firing artillery, armored trains, telegraphy, and mature rail logistics. Compared with the Boer forces, its advantages lay in sustained supply, engineers, and maritime support.

threats.zh: 主要威胁是德兰士瓦与奥兰治自由邦对边境、铁路与莱迪史密斯等要地的进攻，以及内陆防线过长和地方族群紧张。
threats.en: The main threats were attacks by Transvaal and the Orange Free State on the frontier, railways, and key points such as Ladysmith, along with long inland defensive lines and local ethnic tensions.

populationDescription.zh: 纳塔尔人口由祖鲁人和其他非洲社群构成多数，另有白人定居者及不断增长的印度人口。城市集中在沿海与行政中心，广大农村地区仍以传统聚落和种植园劳工社区为主。
populationDescription.en: Natal's population was majority Zulu and other African communities, alongside white settlers and a growing Indian population. Urban concentration existed mainly in the coastal and administrative centers, while much of the countryside remained dominated by traditional settlements and plantation labor communities.

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括英国殖民官员与地主、白人商人和工匠、印度契约劳工与商贩、祖鲁与其他非洲农民和劳工，以及少量受教育的传教学校精英。
socialClasses.en: Main social classes included British colonial officials and landowners, white merchants and artisans, Indian indentured laborers and traders, Zulu and other African farmers and laborers, and a small mission-educated elite.

allies.zh: 主要盟友为大英帝国其他殖民地、开普殖民地以及英国本土军政体系。
allies.en: Its main allies were the rest of the British Empire, the Cape Colony, and the British home military-administrative system.

enemies.zh: 主要敌人为德兰士瓦共和国和奥兰治自由邦的武装力量。
enemies.en: Its principal enemies were the armed forces of the Transvaal Republic and the Orange Free State.

foreignPolicy.zh: 纳塔尔本身缺乏独立外交，但其政策方向服从英国帝国战略，即确保海港、内陆交通和南部非洲的政治主导权。
foreignPolicy.en: Natal lacked an independent foreign policy, but its policy direction followed British imperial strategy: securing ports, inland communications, and political primacy in southern Africa.

era.zh: 成熟工业帝国时代

keyInnovations.zh: 蒸汽港口设施、铁路网络、电报系统、速射炮、装甲列车和现代殖民行政统计。
keyInnovations.en: Steam-powered port facilities, railway networks, telegraph systems, quick-firing artillery, armored trains, and modern colonial administrative statistics.

strengths.zh: 拥有海港、铁路、帝国财政信用和较完善行政体系，是英国南非战区的重要后勤基地。
strengths.en: It possessed ports, railways, imperial fiscal credit, and a relatively developed administrative system, making it a major logistical base for Britain in South Africa.

weaknesses.zh: 社会族群分裂深刻，地方防线拉长，对帝国军援依赖明显，内部政治代表性狭窄。
weaknesses.en: It suffered from deep social and ethnic divisions, extended defensive lines, strong dependence on imperial military support, and narrow internal political representation.

outlook.zh: 凭借英国海权与工业支援，纳塔尔在1900年总体安全前景优于布尔共和国，并将继续融入更大的南非殖民整合进程。
outlook.en: Backed by British sea power and industry, Natal's overall security outlook in 1900 was better than that of the Boer republics, and it was set to become further integrated into the broader colonial consolidation of South Africa.

description.zh: 纳塔尔是依托英国帝国港口、铁路和殖民行政体系而运作、在1900年英布战争中充当关键后方基地的南非殖民地。
description.en: Natal was a South African colony operating through British imperial ports, railways, and colonial administration, serving as a crucial rear base during the Anglo-Boer War in 1900.

dynasty.zh: 巴苏陀王室（莫舒舒家族）

governmentForm.zh: 英国保护地框架下的非洲君主制，由巴苏陀最高酋长在驻地专员监督下统治。
governmentForm.en: An African monarchy under British protectorate oversight, ruled by the Basotho paramount chief under the supervision of a resident commissioner.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以宗族、酋邦与村社为基础，地方酋长在基层治理中仍具重要地位。基督教传播扩大，但传统祖灵信仰、牧牛文化与氏族关系仍深刻影响社会生活。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around clans, chieftaincies, and village communities, with local chiefs retaining major importance in local governance. Christianity was spreading, but ancestral belief systems, cattle culture, and kinship ties continued to shape daily life.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要由莱罗托利及其王室亲族、主要酋长和受传教士教育的新兴书记员构成。英国殖民官员虽人数不多，却掌握对外关系与最终行政裁决权。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted mainly of Lerotholi, the royal kin network, senior chiefs, and a small educated stratum of clerks influenced by missionary schooling. British colonial officials were few in number but held authority over external affairs and final administrative decisions.

succession.zh: 王位通常按王室血统继承，并需得到主要酋长承认。英国当局在继承争议时常介入，以维持保护地稳定。
succession.en: Succession normally followed royal lineage and required recognition by leading chiefs. British authorities often intervened in disputes in order to preserve stability in the protectorate.

structure.zh: 巴苏陀兰的政体结合了传统酋长治理与英国间接统治。最高层为英国驻地专员与其下属行政人员，地方则依靠酋长、头人和 customary courts 维持秩序。
structure.en: Basutoland’s polity combined traditional chiefly authority with British indirect rule. At the top stood the British resident commissioner and subordinate officials, while the local order relied on chiefs, headmen, and customary courts.

function.zh: 统筹行政、对外事务与对酋长体系的监督。
function.en: Oversaw administration, external affairs, and supervision of the chiefly system.

function.zh: 处理酋长任命、习惯法纠纷与地方司法。
function.en: Handled chiefly appointments, customary disputes, and local justice.

function.zh: 征收人头税、管理边境贸易与许可证。
function.en: Collected hut taxes and managed border trade and licensing.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政主要通过酋长与村社头人执行，英国官员通常只在关键地区设巡回监督。山区聚落分散，使地方自治色彩较强。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was carried out chiefly through chiefs and village headmen, with British officers generally exercising itinerant supervision only in key areas. The dispersed mountain settlements gave local governance a strongly autonomous character.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以巴苏陀习惯法为主，重大案件或涉及殖民利益的争端可由英国行政司法机关裁决。刑事与税务案件越来越受殖民程序影响。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based primarily on Basotho customary law, while major cases or disputes involving colonial interests could be decided by British administrative courts. Criminal and tax matters increasingly reflected colonial procedure.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自人头税、牲畜相关征费和贸易许可费。税负不高，但对依赖外出劳工收入的家庭仍有明显压力。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came chiefly from hut taxes, livestock-related levies, and trade license fees. The burden was not extremely high, but it still pressed households dependent on migrant labor income.

religion.zh: 基督教/万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Christianity/animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 19世纪以来，传教学校推动了塞索托语书写、圣经翻译和初等教育传播。巴苏陀口述史、赞歌和牧歌传统在王权记忆与族群认同中占重要地位。
culturalAchievements.en: Since the nineteenth century, mission schools promoted Sesotho literacy, Bible translation, and elementary education. Basotho oral history, praise poetry, and pastoral song traditions remained central to royal memory and ethnic identity.

languageFamily.zh: 班图语系，南班图语支（塞索托语）
languageFamily.en: Bantu language family, Southern Bantu branch (Sesotho)

philosophy.zh: 基督教伦理与巴苏陀传统祖先观、酋长权威和共同体义务并存。
philosophy.en: Christian ethics coexisted with Basotho ancestral traditions, chiefly authority, and communal obligation.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用拉丁字母书写塞索托语与英语。
writingSystem.en: Primarily used the Latin alphabet for Sesotho and English.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业、畜牧和向南非矿区输出劳工汇款为核心。山区环境限制大规模商品农业，但也增强了社会的牧业韧性。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on subsistence farming, livestock herding, and remittances from labor migrants to South African mines. The mountain environment limited large-scale commercial agriculture but also reinforced the resilience of pastoral life.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧、玉米与高粱种植、劳工输出、皮革与手工制品
mainIndustries.en: Livestock, maize and sorghum cultivation, labor migration, leatherwork, and handicrafts

tradeGoods.zh: 牛羊、谷物、羊毛、马匹、皮革、进口布匹与五金
tradeGoods.en: Cattle, sheep, grain, wool, horses, leather, and imported cloth and metal goods

name.zh: 英镑与地方实物交易并用
name.en: Pound sterling with local barter use

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富多以牛群、羊群和谷物储备衡量，现金持有量有限。外出矿工汇款是越来越重要的补充收入来源。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured largely in cattle, sheep, and grain reserves, with limited cash holdings. Remittances from migrant mine workers were an increasingly important supplementary income source.

economicSystem.zh: 以传统村社经济为主，逐步被殖民边境贸易和劳工市场纳入南部非洲区域经济。
economicSystem.en: A predominantly traditional village economy increasingly drawn into the southern African regional economy through colonial border trade and labor markets.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模较小，主要用于缓冲歉收、边境事件或行政开支波动。财政安全很大程度依赖英国保护地体制。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was small and served mainly as a buffer against crop failure, border incidents, or fluctuations in administrative costs. Fiscal security depended heavily on the British protectorate framework.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，追求小幅结余并避免激烈增税，以维持酋长体系与乡村稳定。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative, aiming for modest surpluses and avoiding sharp tax increases so as to preserve the chiefly system and rural stability.

description.zh: 直属最高酋长与主要酋长的常备武装，承担护卫与镇压骚乱任务。
description.en: Permanent armed retainers attached to the paramount chief and major chiefs, used for protection and suppression of disturbances.

description.zh: 由村社与酋邦动员的步骑混合部队，战时集中。
description.en: A mixed infantry-cavalry force mobilized from villages and chieftaincies in times of need.

commanderInChief.zh: 莱罗托利最高酋长（受英国专员监督）

technology.zh: 武器以旧式步枪、少量后装枪和传统长矛为主，缺乏炮兵与现代训练。骑乘和山地机动能力较强。
technology.en: Weapons consisted mainly of older rifles, a limited number of breech-loaders, and traditional spears, with little artillery or modern training. Mounted movement and mountain mobility were comparatively strong.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自周边殖民地压力、边境纠纷及内部酋长继承争端，而非大规模外敌入侵。
threats.en: The main threats came from surrounding colonial pressure, border disputes, and internal chiefly succession conflicts rather than large-scale foreign invasion.

populationDescription.zh: 人口以巴苏陀人为主，聚居于高地村落与河谷耕地。人口分布较分散，但与南非劳工网络联系紧密。
populationDescription.en: The population was overwhelmingly Basotho, concentrated in highland villages and river-valley farmland. Settlement was dispersed, but ties to South African labor networks were close.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与大酋长、小酋长与头人、自由农牧民、外出劳工、少量传教士与书记员。
socialClasses.en: Royal family and senior chiefs, minor chiefs and headmen, free agricultural-pastoral households, migrant laborers, and a small group of missionaries and clerks.

allies.zh: 名义上依附大英帝国，与开普及南部非洲传教网络保持合作。
allies.en: Nominally aligned with the British Empire and cooperative with Cape and southern African missionary networks.

enemies.zh: 无正式国家级敌国，但对布尔势力残余、边境侵占和地区殖民压力保持警惕。
enemies.en: Had no formal state enemy, but remained wary of Boer remnants, border encroachments, and regional colonial pressure.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以依托英国保护、维持内部自治和避免被邻近殖民地吞并为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on relying on British protection, preserving internal autonomy, and avoiding absorption by neighboring colonies.

era.zh: 殖民时代早期的南部非洲山地社会
era.en: Early colonial-era southern African highland society

keyInnovations.zh: 传教学校教育、拉丁字母塞索托文、边境马匹运输、矿业劳工汇款网络。
keyInnovations.en: Mission-school education, Latin-script Sesotho literacy, horse transport across borderlands, and migrant-labor remittance networks.

strengths.zh: 山地地形利于防御，王室与酋长体系具有较高社会动员力，劳工汇款增强了现金流。
strengths.en: Mountainous terrain favored defense, the royal and chiefly system offered substantial social mobilization, and labor remittances improved cash flow.

weaknesses.zh: 财政薄弱，现代武装匮乏，经济对外部矿业市场与英国保护依赖较强。
weaknesses.en: Finances were weak, modern armament was scarce, and the economy depended heavily on external mining markets and British protection.

outlook.zh: 短期内局势较稳，但长期将更深地卷入南部非洲殖民经济与劳工体系。
outlook.en: The short-term outlook was stable, but in the long run it would become more deeply integrated into the colonial economy and labor system of southern Africa.

description.zh: 1900年的巴苏陀兰是在英国保护下保留王室与酋长制度的高地非洲王国。
description.en: In 1900, Basutoland was a highland African kingdom under British protection that preserved its royal and chiefly institutions.

governmentForm.zh: 英国直辖的地中海海军殖民地，由总督代表王权统治。
governmentForm.en: A British Crown colony in the Mediterranean, governed by a governor representing imperial sovereignty.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由英属殖民官僚、马耳他教士与专业阶层、港口工人、小商人和农村佃农构成。城市人口比重较高，瓦莱塔与三城地区围绕军港、船坞与服务业形成密集社会网络。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of British colonial officials, Maltese clergy and professionals, dock workers, small traders, and rural tenant farmers. Urbanization was relatively high, and Valletta with the Three Cities formed a dense social network around the naval dockyard, port, and service sectors.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要是英国总督、驻军高级军官与殖民文官，同时马耳他天主教会和本地法律、商业精英具有重要影响。地方名流在咨询机构中扮演中介角色。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was composed chiefly of the British governor, senior military officers, and colonial civil servants, while the Maltese Catholic Church and local legal and commercial elites retained major influence. Local notables acted as intermediaries within consultative institutions.

succession.zh: 总督由英国政府任命，不实行本地王位继承。地方政治代表与议政机构的组成受殖民宪制调整影响较大。
succession.en: The governor was appointed by the British government, with no local dynastic succession. The composition of local representative bodies depended heavily on changing colonial constitutional arrangements.

structure.zh: 马耳他由总督、殖民秘书及各专业部门治理，海军与陆军利益在行政中占据核心地位。有限的地方代表机构存在，但关键财政、军务与港口事务由英国掌握。
structure.en: Malta was governed through the governor, colonial secretary, and specialized departments, with naval and military interests at the center of administration. Limited representative institutions existed, but key matters of finance, defense, and the harbor remained under British control.

function.zh: 统筹全岛行政、立法协调及与伦敦沟通。
function.en: Coordinated island administration, legislative affairs, and communication with London.

function.zh: 管理军港、修船设施、补给和港口秩序。
function.en: Managed the naval harbor, repair facilities, supplies, and port order.

function.zh: 征收关税、消费税并管理殖民预算。
function.en: Collected customs and excise duties and administered the colonial budget.

name.en: Public Works and Public Health Department

function.zh: 负责道路、供水、防疫和城市设施维护。
function.en: Handled roads, water supply, disease control, and maintenance of urban infrastructure.

localAdmin.zh: 地方管理集中于岛屿尺度，市镇委员会负责部分民政与卫生事务。由于领土狭小，行政层级较少而执行效率较高。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized on an island-wide basis, with municipal bodies handling portions of civil and sanitary affairs. Because the territory was compact, administrative layers were relatively few and execution was comparatively efficient.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系融合罗马法传统、圣约翰骑士团遗产与英国殖民法。商法、海事法与刑事程序受英国影响尤深。
legalSystem.en: The legal system blended Roman-law tradition, the legacy of the Knights of St. John, and British colonial law. Commercial law, maritime law, and criminal procedure were especially influenced by Britain.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要依靠关税、消费税、港口收费和地产相关征收。由于军港经济重要，间接税占较大比重。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied mainly on customs duties, excise taxes, port fees, and property-related levies. Given the importance of the naval base economy, indirect taxation formed a large share of revenue.

religion.zh: 天主教
religion.en: Catholicism

culturalAchievements.zh: 马耳他在教堂建筑、要塞工程、海事文化和多语文学传统方面具有显著积累。19世纪末教育、印刷业和报刊发展推动了马耳他语公共文化的成长。
culturalAchievements.en: Malta possessed notable achievements in church architecture, fortification engineering, maritime culture, and multilingual literary traditions. By the late nineteenth century, education, printing, and newspapers were helping to expand a Maltese public culture.

languageFamily.zh: 马耳他语属闪米特语族阿拉伯语支，并长期受意大利语和英语影响
languageFamily.en: Maltese belongs to the Semitic family, Arabic branch, and was long influenced by Italian and English

philosophy.zh: 以天主教社会伦理为核心，并受英国行政理性与地中海城市文化影响。
philosophy.en: Centered on Catholic social ethics while also shaped by British administrative rationality and Mediterranean urban culture.

writingSystem.zh: 使用拉丁字母，行政中通行英语与意大利语，马耳他语书面化程度不断提高。
writingSystem.en: Used the Latin alphabet; English and Italian were common in administration, while written Maltese was becoming increasingly standardized.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济高度依赖英国海军基地、船坞修理、港口转运和驻军消费。农业规模有限，工业以与海运和军需相关的修理、加工和服务业为主。
gdpDescription.en: The economy depended heavily on the British naval base, dockyard repair work, port transshipment, and garrison spending. Agriculture was limited, and industry centered on repair, processing, and services linked to shipping and military needs.

mainIndustries.zh: 船坞修理、港口服务、军需供应、建筑、石材开采、少量农业
mainIndustries.en: Dockyard repair, port services, military supply, construction, stone quarrying, and limited agriculture

tradeGoods.zh: 船舶补给、煤炭转运、石材、葡萄酒、土豆、进口粮食与制造品
tradeGoods.en: Naval supplies, coal transshipment, stone, wine, potatoes, and imported grain and manufactures

householdWealth.zh: 城市工人家庭收入与军港景气密切相关，中产阶层主要来自法律、教会、商贸和行政服务。农村家庭土地有限，对粮食价格较为敏感。
householdWealth.en: Urban working-class household income was closely tied to the fortunes of the naval port, while the middle class came mainly from law, the Church, commerce, and administration. Rural families held limited land and were sensitive to food prices.

economicSystem.zh: 以殖民港口资本主义和军港服务经济为主，深度嵌入英国地中海战略网络。
economicSystem.en: A colonial port-capitalist and naval-service economy deeply integrated into Britain’s Mediterranean strategic network.

treasuryDescription.zh: 殖民财政较稳健，且在必要时可获得帝国海军与财政支持。由于经济高度依赖军港，财政也容易受战略调整影响。
treasuryDescription.en: Colonial finances were relatively sound and could draw upon imperial naval and fiscal backing when required. However, dependence on the naval base meant revenues were vulnerable to strategic changes.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策注重维持港口与市政设施运转，避免过重直接税，以保障军港竞争力与社会稳定。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized keeping port and municipal infrastructure functioning while avoiding excessive direct taxation so as to preserve the naval base’s competitiveness and social stability.

description.zh: 负责岛上防御、炮台守备和治安支援。
description.en: Responsible for island defense, fortification manning, and support to internal security.

name.zh: 皇家海军驻马耳他分遣队

description.zh: 依托大港设施执行补给、维修与地中海舰队支援任务。
description.en: Used Grand Harbour facilities for supply, repair, and support to the Mediterranean Fleet.

name.zh: 马耳他民兵与警备部队

description.zh: 由本地人员组成，承担辅助守备、勤务和部分岸防任务。
description.en: Locally recruited forces used for auxiliary garrison, service, and some coastal defense duties.

commanderInChief.zh: 英国总督兼驻军高级司令体系
commanderInChief.en: British Governor within the imperial garrison command structure

technology.zh: 拥有近代化港口炮台、蒸汽舰维修设施、电报和现代步枪，是地中海重要军事节点。
technology.en: Possessed modern harbor fortifications, steamship repair facilities, telegraph connections, and modern rifles, making it a major military node in the Mediterranean.

threats.zh: 主要威胁是列强海军竞争、地中海战争风险和港口封锁，而非岛内大规模叛乱。
threats.en: The main threats were great-power naval rivalry, the risk of Mediterranean war, and blockade of the harbor rather than large-scale domestic rebellion.

populationDescription.zh: 人口稠密，多数为马耳他天主教徒，集中于港口城市群与若干农村村镇。英国军人、官员与外来商人构成显著但少数的外籍群体。
populationDescription.en: The population was dense and overwhelmingly Maltese Catholic, concentrated in the harbor conurbation and several rural towns and villages. British soldiers, officials, and foreign merchants formed a visible but minority expatriate population.

socialClasses.zh: 英国官僚与军官、教士与专业中产、商人与店主、船坞工人与港口劳工、农村佃农与小农。
socialClasses.en: British officials and officers, clergy and professional middle classes, merchants and shopkeepers, dockyard and port workers, and rural tenants and smallholders.

allies.zh: 作为英国殖民地，外交上属于大英帝国体系。
allies.en: As a British colony, its diplomatic alignment was that of the British Empire.

enemies.zh: 无独立外交敌国；潜在敌对对象主要是与英国竞争的地中海海军强国。
enemies.en: It had no independent diplomatic enemy; potential adversaries were primarily Mediterranean naval powers competing with Britain.

foreignPolicy.zh: 无自主外交，战略功能是保障英国通往苏伊士和印度洋航线的地中海中枢。
foreignPolicy.en: It possessed no independent foreign policy; its strategic function was to secure Britain’s Mediterranean hub on the route to Suez and the Indian Ocean.

era.zh: 高帝国主义时期的蒸汽海军殖民据点

keyInnovations.zh: 蒸汽舰船坞维修、海底电报、现代岸炮、海军煤站与港口卫生工程。
keyInnovations.en: Steamship dockyard repair, submarine telegraph links, modern coastal artillery, naval coaling stations, and port sanitary engineering.

strengths.zh: 港口优越、军事实力强、行政效率高、受帝国资源支持。
strengths.en: It possessed an excellent harbor, strong military power, efficient administration, and support from imperial resources.

weaknesses.zh: 经济结构单一，粮食依赖进口，本地政治自主权有限。
weaknesses.en: Its economy was narrow, food supply depended on imports, and local political autonomy was limited.

outlook.zh: 只要英国海军优势维持，马耳他将继续作为地中海关键军港和补给中心繁荣。
outlook.en: So long as British naval supremacy endured, Malta was likely to remain a prosperous key naval and supply center in the Mediterranean.

description.zh: 1900年的马耳他是英国在地中海的核心军港殖民地，兼具强大要塞、港口经济与深厚天主教文化。
description.en: In 1900, Malta was Britain’s core naval colony in the Mediterranean, combining powerful fortifications, a port economy, and deep Catholic cultural traditions.

dynasty.zh: 西班牙波旁王朝殖民统治

governmentForm.zh: 由西班牙总督统辖的殖民地行政体制，依靠沿海据点和有限内陆控制实施统治。
governmentForm.en: A colonial administrative system governed by a Spanish governor, ruling through coastal posts and limited inland control.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由岛上与沿海的非洲族群、少数西班牙官员与商人、传教士以及受殖民教育影响的中间群体构成。内陆地区许多社群仍按传统首领、亲属网络和村落权威组织生活。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of African peoples on the islands and coast, a small number of Spanish officials and traders, missionaries, and a modest intermediary stratum shaped by colonial education. Many inland communities still lived under traditional chiefs, kinship ties, and village authority.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要是西班牙总督、殖民军官、传教会代表和依附殖民贸易的地方头人。欧洲人数量很少，但在军事、司法和海关上垄断权力。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was made up mainly of the Spanish governor, colonial officers, mission representatives, and local headmen tied to colonial trade. Europeans were few in number, but they monopolized military, judicial, and customs authority.

succession.zh: 总督由西班牙政府任命，不存在本地统一王位继承。各族社群内部仍多按习惯法和家族权威处理首领继承。
succession.en: The governor was appointed by the Spanish government, and there was no unified local dynastic succession. Within individual communities, leadership succession still followed customary law and family authority.

structure.zh: 行政中心位于圣伊萨贝尔，治理重点在费尔南多波岛和若干沿海站点。殖民政府人力薄弱，常依赖传教团体、商站与土著头人间接管理腹地。
structure.en: The administrative center was at Santa Isabel, with governance focused on Fernando Pó and several coastal stations. Colonial manpower was thin, so the government often relied on missions, trading posts, and indigenous headmen to manage the hinterland indirectly.

function.zh: 统筹殖民行政、治安和对马德里的报告。
function.en: Directed colonial administration, public order, and reporting to Madrid.

function.zh: 征收进出口税、港口费用并管理预算。
function.en: Collected import-export duties, port fees, and administered the budget.

function.zh: 负责港口警备、巡逻和有限的军事执行。
function.en: Handled port security, patrols, and limited military enforcement.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政覆盖不均，岛屿和沿海据点控制较强，内陆多依靠巡回官员和地方首领协助。殖民命令在雨林腹地的实际执行力有限。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was uneven: control was stronger on the islands and coastal stations, while the interior depended on itinerant officers and cooperation from local leaders. Colonial orders had limited practical reach in the rainforest hinterland.

legalSystem.zh: 法律上适用西班牙殖民法规，但在多数土著社群中仍广泛容纳习惯法。涉及贸易、治安和传教事务时，西班牙行政司法优先。
legalSystem.en: Spanish colonial regulations applied in law, but customary law remained widely tolerated in most indigenous communities. In matters of trade, public order, and mission activity, Spanish administrative justice took precedence.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以关税、港口收费和商业许可为主，直接深入内陆征税能力有限。部分地区通过劳役、贸易控制和契约劳动间接汲取资源。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from customs duties, port charges, and commercial licenses, as the state had limited capacity to tax the interior directly. In some areas resources were extracted indirectly through labor obligations, trade control, and contract labor.

religion.zh: 天主教
religion.en: Catholicism

culturalAchievements.zh: 19世纪末，天主教传教站推动了基础教育、教会建筑和若干本地语言记录。与此同时，芳族、布比族等社群的口述传统、音乐和仪式文化仍是社会认同的核心。
culturalAchievements.en: By the late nineteenth century, Catholic mission stations promoted elementary education, church building, and the recording of some local languages. At the same time, the oral traditions, music, and ritual cultures of groups such as the Fang and Bubi remained central to social identity.

languageFamily.zh: 多语言地区，主要包括班图语系诸语言及西班牙语
languageFamily.en: A multilingual region, mainly including Bantu languages and Spanish

philosophy.zh: 天主教传教思想与当地祖灵信仰、氏族传统和仪式生活并存。
philosophy.en: Catholic missionary ideology coexisted with local ancestral beliefs, clan traditions, and ritual life.

writingSystem.zh: 行政和传教中使用拉丁字母书写西班牙语及部分本地语言。
writingSystem.en: The Latin alphabet was used in administration and missions for Spanish and some local languages.

unit.zh: 西班牙银比塞塔折算（约当时价值）

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模较小，依托热带农产品、木材、港口贸易和有限种植园开发。殖民地的商业活动主要集中于岛屿和沿海，腹地经济仍多为村社农业与采集。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was small, based on tropical produce, timber, port trade, and limited plantation development. Commercial activity was concentrated on the islands and coast, while the hinterland remained dominated by village agriculture and gathering.

mainIndustries.zh: 可可种植、棕榈制品、木材采集、港口贸易、传教附属手工业
mainIndustries.en: Cocoa cultivation, palm products, timber extraction, port trade, and mission-associated handicrafts

tradeGoods.zh: 可可、棕榈油、木材、象牙少量、橡胶少量、进口纺织品与酒类
tradeGoods.en: Cocoa, palm oil, timber, small amounts of ivory, small amounts of rubber, and imported textiles and alcohol

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富体现在土地使用权、农作物、家屋与亲属网络，而非大量现金。沿海和岛上与殖民贸易接轨的群体现金收入更高。
householdWealth.en: For most households, wealth lay in land use rights, crops, dwellings, and kinship networks rather than large cash holdings. Coastal and island groups connected to colonial trade had higher cash incomes.

economicSystem.zh: 沿海殖民贸易经济与内陆村社自给经济并存，资源开发程度仍较初步。
economicSystem.en: A coexistence of coastal colonial trade economy and inland village subsistence economy, with resource exploitation still at an early stage.

treasuryDescription.zh: 殖民财政储备有限，经常依赖宗主国补助和延后公共支出。贸易波动或疾病流行都可能迅速削弱财政能力。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury reserves were limited, and the colony often depended on metropolitan subsidies and postponed public spending. Trade fluctuations or disease outbreaks could quickly weaken its fiscal capacity.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持最低限度殖民存在为目标，优先保障港口、警备和行政运作。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed at maintaining a minimum viable colonial presence, prioritizing ports, security, and basic administration.

description.zh: 驻守圣伊萨贝尔及主要据点，用于守备与巡逻。
description.en: Stationed at Santa Isabel and major posts for garrison duty and patrol.

name.zh: 港口警备与海岸巡逻队

description.zh: 负责海岸治安、缉私和港口控制。
description.en: Responsible for coastal security, anti-smuggling work, and port control.

description.zh: 由本地招募人员组成，承担向导、搬运、警戒和战时支援。
description.en: Locally recruited personnel used for guiding, transport, guarding, and wartime support.

commanderInChief.zh: 西班牙总督兼殖民守备最高负责人
commanderInChief.en: Spanish Governor as supreme head of colonial defense

technology.zh: 装备以步枪、小型炮和蒸汽船支援为主，通信和运输能力有限，难以长期深入内陆作战。
technology.en: Equipment centered on rifles, light artillery, and some steamship support, but communications and transport were limited, making sustained inland operations difficult.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自热带疾病、内陆控制不足、劳工与贸易冲突，以及列强在中西非的殖民竞争。
threats.en: The main threats were tropical disease, weak inland control, labor and trade conflict, and colonial competition among powers in west-central Africa.

populationDescription.zh: 人口以布比族、芳族及其他本地社群为主，欧洲与克里奥尔人口比例很低。人口主要分散在村落、沿海据点与岛上聚居地。
populationDescription.en: The population consisted mainly of Bubi, Fang, and other local communities, with Europeans and Creoles forming only a small minority. Most people lived in villages, coastal stations, and island settlements.

socialClasses.zh: 西班牙官员与商人、传教士、地方首领、沿海工人和搬运者、广大村社农民与采集者。
socialClasses.en: Spanish officials and traders, missionaries, local chiefs, coastal laborers and carriers, and a broad base of village cultivators and gatherers.

allies.zh: 作为西班牙殖民地，外交上从属于西班牙。
allies.en: As a Spanish colony, it was diplomatically subordinate to Spain.

enemies.zh: 无独立正式敌国，但与邻近法属、德属殖民势力存在潜在边界和影响力竞争。
enemies.en: It had no independent formal enemy, but there was latent rivalry with neighboring French and German colonial powers over boundaries and influence.

foreignPolicy.zh: 无自主外交，殖民目标是巩固沿海控制、扩大经济开发并在列强瓜分非洲格局中维持西班牙存在。
foreignPolicy.en: It had no autonomous foreign policy; colonial aims were to consolidate coastal control, expand economic exploitation, and preserve Spain’s presence amid the partition of Africa.

era.zh: 帝国主义时期的中非热带殖民前哨
era.en: A tropical central African colonial outpost in the age of imperialism

keyInnovations.zh: 蒸汽海运补给、传教学校教育、港口仓储、初步种植园技术和拉丁字母记录本地语言。
keyInnovations.en: Steamship supply links, mission-school education, port warehousing, early plantation techniques, and Latin-script documentation of local languages.

strengths.zh: 拥有沿海据点和岛屿港口，可发展热带经济作物，并受宗主国海权支援。
strengths.en: It possessed coastal stations and island ports, could develop tropical cash crops, and benefited from metropolitan seaborne support.

weaknesses.zh: 行政人手稀少，财政不足，疾病严重，内陆控制薄弱。
weaknesses.en: It suffered from scarce administrative manpower, inadequate finances, severe disease burdens, and weak inland control.

outlook.zh: 短期内可维持稳定的殖民据点，但若无更大资本与基础设施投入，其发展仍将缓慢且局限于海岸与岛屿。
outlook.en: In the short term it could remain a stable colonial foothold, but without larger capital and infrastructure investment its development would stay slow and largely confined to the coast and islands.

description.zh: 1900年的西属几内亚是西班牙在中西非维持的边缘殖民地，控制集中于岛屿港口与少数沿海站点。
description.en: In 1900, Spanish Guinea was a peripheral Spanish colony in west-central Africa, with control concentrated in island ports and a few coastal stations.

governmentForm.zh: 汤加在1900年是由图普王朝统治的君主立宪王国，王权强于议会但已受成文宪法约束。
governmentForm.en: In 1900, Tonga was a constitutional monarchy ruled by the Tupou dynasty, with royal authority stronger than parliament but constrained by a written constitution.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由国王、贵族、自由民与教会阶层构成，传统首领体系与基督教制度并存。19世纪后期土地改革削弱了部分旧氏族权力，但贵族仍在地方社会中占据核心地位。
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around the king, nobles, commoners, and church elites, with traditional chiefly organization coexisting with Christian institutions. Late nineteenth-century reforms weakened some older clan powers, but the nobility still held a central role in local society.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要由国王、世袭贵族与受教育的教会及文官精英组成。卫理公会背景的政治与宗教人物对国家决策影响显著。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted chiefly of the king, hereditary nobles, and educated church and civil elites. Political and religious figures linked to the Methodist sphere exercised notable influence over state policy.

succession.zh: 王位依图普王朝的世袭原则传承，并受宪法确认。继承通常优先王室直系血统，以保障政权稳定。
succession.en: The throne passed hereditarily within the Tupou dynasty under principles recognized by the constitution. Succession generally favored the direct royal line to preserve political stability.

structure.zh: 中央政府以国王为核心，设有内阁、大臣与立法机构。英国保护关系于1900年开始形成，但王国内政仍大体由本国政府处理。
structure.en: The central government was centered on the king and included a cabinet, ministers, and a legislative body. A British protectorate relationship began in 1900, but domestic affairs were still largely managed by the Tongan government.

function.zh: 协调国王诏令、内阁运作与对外联络。
function.en: Coordinated royal decrees, cabinet operations, and external communications.

function.zh: 管理土地分配、地方行政、人口登记与治安事务。
function.en: Managed land allocation, local administration, population registration, and internal order.

function.zh: 征收关税、处理国库收支并监督港口贸易。
function.en: Collected customs duties, managed treasury income and expenditure, and supervised port trade.

function.zh: 负责法院、监狱、警察与宪法法令执行。
function.en: Oversaw courts, prisons, police, and enforcement of constitutional law.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依群岛与村社组织展开，由贵族、地方官与教会人士共同维持秩序。各岛在王权名义下保留一定传统管理惯例。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through island and village structures, with nobles, local officials, and church figures jointly maintaining order. The islands retained some traditional governing practices under royal authority.

legalSystem.zh: 法律制度以1875年宪法、王室法令与基督教化的习惯法为基础。正式法院逐渐取代纯粹酋长裁断。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on the 1875 constitution, royal decrees, and Christianized customary law. Formal courts increasingly replaced purely chiefly adjudication.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以关税、土地相关征收及若干人头或劳役义务替代形式为主。由于经济规模有限，国家财政高度依赖进口税与港口收费。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied mainly on customs duties, land-related levies, and some commuted poll or labor obligations. Because the economy was small, state finance depended heavily on import duties and port fees.

religion.zh: 基督教
religion.en: Christianity

culturalAchievements.zh: 19世纪的汤加在圣经翻译、教会教育与成文宪法方面取得显著成就。传统歌舞、口述史与礼仪文化在基督教时代仍被积极保存。
culturalAchievements.en: Nineteenth-century Tonga achieved notable advances in Bible translation, church education, and constitutional codification. Traditional dance, oral history, and ceremonial culture were actively preserved in the Christian era.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系波利尼西亚语族
languageFamily.en: Austronesian, Polynesian branch

philosophy.zh: 汤加文化以波利尼西亚王权传统、基督教伦理与家族义务观念为核心。
philosophy.en: Tongan culture was grounded in Polynesian kingship traditions, Christian ethics, and strong ideas of familial obligation.

writingSystem.zh: 拉丁字母书写的汤加语与英语并用。
writingSystem.en: Tongan written in the Latin alphabet was used alongside English.

unit.en: pounds sterling silver-equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 汤加经济以自给农业、椰干出口和沿海贸易为主，货币化程度有限。整体规模很小，但土地制度较稳定，足以维持王国运作。
gdpDescription.en: Tonga's economy was based on subsistence agriculture, copra exports, and coastal trade, with only limited monetization. The overall scale was very small, but its land system was stable enough to sustain the kingdom.

mainIndustries.zh: 椰干、芋头与薯类种植、渔业、手工编织、小规模船运
mainIndustries.en: Copra production, taro and root-crop farming, fishing, handicrafts, and small-scale shipping

tradeGoods.zh: 出口椰干、香蕉、香草少量产品；进口纺织品、金属器具、面粉、煤油
tradeGoods.en: Exports included copra, bananas, and small quantities of vanilla; imports included textiles, metal goods, flour, and kerosene

name.en: Pound sterling and foreign silver coinage

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富多体现在土地使用权、椰林、独木舟、牲畜和礼仪物品上，现金储蓄较少。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly through land-use rights, coconut groves, canoes, livestock, and ceremonial goods, with little cash savings.

unit.en: pounds sterling silver-equivalent per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系为王国法律保护下的村社农业与港口贸易混合体，传统再分配与市场交换并行。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a hybrid of village agriculture and port trade under royal law, with traditional redistribution operating alongside market exchange.

category.zh: 教育、教会协作与公共工程
category.en: Education, church cooperation, and public works

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库存量不大，但在平稳年份足以应对短期进口采购和行政周转。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was modest, but in normal years it was sufficient for short-term import purchases and administrative cash flow.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，强调收支平衡、避免过度负债，并以关税维持国家机器。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative, emphasizing balanced budgets, avoidance of heavy debt, and maintenance of the state through customs revenue.

description.zh: 负责王室安全、首都驻防与礼仪任务。
description.en: Responsible for royal security, garrison duty in the capital, and ceremonial functions.

description.zh: 由各岛可动员男子组成，用于治安与紧急防御。
description.en: Composed of mobilizable men from the islands for policing and emergency defense.

description.zh: 使用少量船只执行近海联络与巡查。
description.en: Used a small number of craft for coastal liaison and patrol.

commanderInChief.zh: 乔治·图普二世国王

technology.zh: 装备以步枪、猎枪和少量轻武器为主，缺乏现代火炮与正规海军设施。训练水平有限，但王室卫队纪律相对较好。
technology.en: Equipment consisted mainly of rifles, shotguns, and a small number of light weapons, with no modern artillery or formal naval infrastructure. Training was limited, though the royal guard was relatively disciplined.

threats.zh: 主要威胁并非邻近入侵，而是列强干预、海上封锁和内部政治派系摩擦。
threats.en: The chief threats were not nearby invasion but great-power intervention, maritime pressure, and internal political factionalism.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要为波利尼西亚汤加人，少量欧洲传教士、商人与混血社群集中在港口地区。基督教已成为几乎普遍的宗教认同。
populationDescription.en: The population was predominantly Polynesian Tongan, with small communities of European missionaries, traders, and mixed-descent residents concentrated in port areas. Christianity had become an almost universal religious identity.

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层为王室、贵族、自由民、教职人员与少量外来商人。
socialClasses.en: The main social strata were the royal house, nobles, commoners, clergy, and a small foreign merchant community.

allies.zh: 与英国保持保护关系，并与斐济、萨摩亚等太平洋殖民港口维持贸易联系。
allies.en: It maintained a protectorate relationship with Britain and trading links with colonial ports such as Fiji and Samoa.

enemies.zh: 无明确传统宿敌，但对德国、美国等列强在南太平洋的扩张保持警惕。
enemies.en: It had no clear traditional enemy, but remained wary of German and American expansion in the South Pacific.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以在列强夹缝中保存王国自治为核心，倾向依靠英国保护来避免直接吞并。
foreignPolicy.en: Its foreign policy centered on preserving autonomy amid imperial rivalry, leaning on British protection to avoid outright annexation.

era.zh: 晚期前工业—早期殖民接触时代
era.en: Late pre-industrial to early colonial-contact era

keyInnovations.zh: 关键变化包括宪法制度、教会学校、印刷识字传播、蒸汽船联系与近代港口行政。
keyInnovations.en: Key developments included constitutional institutions, church schools, the spread of print literacy, steamship connections, and modern port administration.

strengths.zh: 政治合法性较强，社会凝聚力高，土地制度稳定，并成功保留形式上的本土主权。
strengths.en: It possessed strong political legitimacy, high social cohesion, a stable land system, and had successfully preserved formal indigenous sovereignty.

weaknesses.zh: 经济规模极小，财政脆弱，军事力量有限，对外贸易和外部保护依赖较深。
weaknesses.en: Its economy was extremely small, finances fragile, military power limited, and dependence on foreign trade and external protection was significant.

outlook.zh: 短期内可维持稳定，但长期命运仍受英国保护体制与太平洋帝国竞争左右。
outlook.en: It could remain stable in the short term, but its long-term fate would still be shaped by the British protectorate framework and imperial rivalry in the Pacific.

description.zh: 1900年的汤加是一个以图普王朝和基督教秩序为核心、在英国保护下维持自治的小型波利尼西亚王国。
description.en: In 1900, Tonga was a small Polynesian kingdom centered on the Tupou dynasty and Christian order, preserving autonomy under British protection.

dynasty.zh: 德属萨摩亚殖民政府时期（名义上保留部分萨摩亚酋邦传统）
dynasty.en: German Samoa colonial administration period (with nominal survival of some Samoan chiefly traditions)

governmentForm.zh: 1900年的萨摩亚为德国保护下的殖民地行政体，由总督统治并部分利用本地酋长结构。
governmentForm.en: In 1900, Samoa was a colonial administrative territory under German protection, governed by a governor and partly supported by indigenous chiefly structures.

socialStructure.zh: 社会仍以萨摩亚扩大家族、村社与酋长制度为基础，德国殖民当局则在其上叠加官僚统治。欧洲商人、种植园管理者和传教士在沿海城镇拥有较高地位。
socialStructure.en: Society remained based on Samoan extended families, village communities, and chiefly institutions, over which the German colonial regime imposed bureaucratic rule. European merchants, plantation managers, and missionaries held elevated status in coastal towns.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由德国总督及其官员、欧洲商贸种植利益集团与部分合作的本地高阶酋长组成。殖民国家权力最终压倒本地王权形式。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was composed of the German governor and officials, European commercial and plantation interests, and some cooperating high-ranking indigenous chiefs. Colonial state power ultimately outweighed indigenous royal forms.

succession.zh: 本地酋长头衔依家族与传统协商继承，但最高政治权力由德意志帝国任命总督掌握。1899年后原有王位争议事实上被殖民政制取代。
succession.en: Indigenous chiefly titles passed through family custom and negotiation, but supreme political authority rested with a governor appointed by the German Empire. After 1899, earlier disputes over kingship were effectively superseded by colonial rule.

structure.zh: 政府以阿皮亚的总督府为中心，下设财政、警务、司法和种植园管理职能。德属萨摩亚在形式上整合群岛，但对村社日常事务常通过本地首领间接统治。
structure.en: Government was centered on the governor's office in Apia, with functions for finance, policing, justice, and plantation administration. German Samoa formally unified the islands, but village affairs were often managed indirectly through local chiefs.

function.zh: 统筹殖民政策、对外事务、行政命令与本地酋长关系。
function.en: Directed colonial policy, external affairs, administrative decrees, and relations with local chiefs.

function.zh: 管理关税、港口收入、特许经营与预算。
function.en: Managed customs, port revenue, concessions, and the budget.

function.zh: 负责警察、地方法院、劳工纠纷与治安。
function.en: Oversaw police, local courts, labor disputes, and public order.

function.zh: 监督土地登记、种植园经营、劳工调配及出口作物生产。
function.en: Supervised land registration, plantation operations, labor allocation, and export-crop production.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托村社首领、传教网络和殖民驻地官员共同运行。德国当局倾向保留村社框架，但在税收、治安和劳动管理上施加强力控制。
localAdmin.en: Local governance functioned through village chiefs, missionary networks, and colonial district officers. The German authorities tended to preserve the village framework while imposing strong control over taxation, order, and labor management.

legalSystem.zh: 法律制度为德国殖民法令与萨摩亚习惯法并存。涉及土地、家族和头衔的事务常保留本地规范，而商业与刑事案件则更多由殖民法院处理。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined German colonial ordinances with Samoan customary law. Matters involving land, family, and chiefly titles often retained indigenous norms, while commercial and criminal cases were more often handled by colonial courts.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以关税、营业许可、港口收费和种植出口相关征收为主。村社亦可能承担劳役或行政配合义务。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied on customs, business licenses, port fees, and levies tied to plantation exports. Village communities could also bear labor or administrative obligations.

religion.zh: 基督教
religion.en: Christianity

culturalAchievements.zh: 萨摩亚在传教教育、识字推广和赞美诗传统方面发展突出。尽管处于殖民统治之下，纹身、礼仪交换和口述传统仍持续维系社会认同。
culturalAchievements.en: Samoa saw notable development in missionary education, literacy, and hymn traditions. Despite colonial rule, tattooing, ceremonial exchange, and oral tradition continued to sustain social identity.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系波利尼西亚语族
languageFamily.en: Austronesian, Polynesian branch

philosophy.zh: 萨摩亚文化核心是fa'a Samoa，即以家族、酋长、礼仪与集体责任为中心的生活秩序，并受到基督教深刻塑造。
philosophy.en: The core of Samoan culture was fa'a Samoa, a way of life centered on family, chiefs, ceremony, and collective responsibility, deeply reshaped by Christianity.

writingSystem.zh: 萨摩亚语使用拉丁字母，德语与英语亦见于行政和商业领域。
writingSystem.en: Samoan was written in the Latin alphabet, while German and English also appeared in administration and commerce.

unit.en: German gold marks equivalent per year
German gold marks equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 德属萨摩亚经济比多数小型太平洋群岛更具出口导向，椰干和可可种植园具有重要地位。阿皮亚作为贸易港口使货币经济渗透程度高于纯粹村社经济。
gdpDescription.en: German Samoa had a more export-oriented economy than many small Pacific island polities, with copra and cocoa plantations playing major roles. Apia's position as a trade port meant monetized exchange penetrated more deeply than in a purely village economy.

mainIndustries.zh: 椰干、可可、橡胶试种、港口贸易、船舶补给、手工业
mainIndustries.en: Copra, cocoa, experimental rubber cultivation, port trade, ship provisioning, and handicrafts

tradeGoods.zh: 出口椰干、可可、椰子制品；进口布匹、工具、酒类、建筑材料、日用品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included copra, cocoa, and coconut products; imports included cloth, tools, alcohol, building materials, and consumer goods

name.zh: 德国金马克与外来硬币并用
name.en: German gold mark and foreign coinage

householdWealth.zh: 本地家庭财富多体现为家族土地使用、椰林、住房、渔具与礼物网络；欧洲居民则更多以现金、商号和地产计量财富。
householdWealth.en: For indigenous households, wealth was expressed through family land use, coconut groves, housing, fishing gear, and gift networks; for Europeans it was measured more in cash, firms, and real estate.

unit.en: German gold marks equivalent per person per year
German gold marks equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系是殖民种植园资本主义与萨摩亚村社生产并存的混合结构。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a hybrid structure in which colonial plantation capitalism coexisted with Samoan village production.

unit.en: German gold marks equivalent per year
German gold marks equivalent per year
German gold marks equivalent per year

source.zh: 土地、种植园与出口征收

treasuryDescription.zh: 殖民财政储备有限，但因有德国帝国背景与商业网络支撑，流动性较一般岛屿政体更强。
treasuryDescription.en: Colonial cash reserves were limited, but backed by imperial German support and commercial networks, liquidity was stronger than in most island polities.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以自给型殖民预算为目标，优先保障港口、治安与出口产业行政需求。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed at a self-supporting colonial budget, prioritizing ports, security, and administration for export industries.

description.zh: 维持阿皮亚及主要岛屿治安，是事实上的主要武装力量。
description.en: Maintained order in Apia and the main islands and served as the de facto principal armed force.

description.zh: 由本地人员组成，用于巡逻、押解和应急动员。
description.en: Made up of local personnel for patrol, escort, and emergency mobilization.

description.zh: 负责阿皮亚港巡查与船只联络，不具独立海军能力。
description.en: Handled patrol duties and vessel liaison in Apia harbor, without constituting an independent navy.

commanderInChief.zh: 总督威廉·索尔夫

technology.zh: 武装水平主要为殖民警察步枪、左轮手枪和少量机关枪支持，海防依赖帝国海军偶发存在。通信与运输依靠蒸汽船和电报联系。
technology.en: Armament consisted mainly of police rifles, revolvers, and limited machine-gun support, while naval defense depended on occasional imperial naval presence. Communications and transport relied on steamship and telegraphic links.

unit.en: German gold marks equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自列强竞争遗留影响、本地反殖民不满和劳工秩序问题，而非大规模外敌入侵。
threats.en: The principal threats came from the legacy of great-power rivalry, local anti-colonial discontent, and labor-order issues rather than large-scale external invasion.

populationDescription.zh: 人口以萨摩亚人为绝大多数，另有德国人、其他欧洲人、华工及少量混血居民。阿皮亚是最明显的多族群港口中心。
populationDescription.en: The population was overwhelmingly Samoan, with Germans, other Europeans, Chinese laborers, and a small mixed-descent community also present. Apia was the clearest multiethnic port center.

socialClasses.zh: 主要包括酋长与家族长、普通村民、教职人员、殖民官员、商人和契约劳工。
socialClasses.en: The main social groups included chiefs and family heads, ordinary villagers, clergy, colonial officials, merchants, and indentured laborers.

allies.zh: 作为德属殖民地，其主要依靠德意志帝国，并与德属太平洋据点保持联系。
allies.en: As a German colony, it relied primarily on the German Empire and maintained links with other German Pacific possessions.

enemies.zh: 无正式本地国家敌手，但与美国属萨摩亚存在分治格局，对英国影响亦保持警惕。
enemies.en: It had no formal local state enemy, but existed in a partitioned arrangement alongside American Samoa and remained alert to British influence.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策由德国帝国决定，目标是在维持秩序的同时确保种植园经济和海上战略通道。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was determined by the German Empire, aiming to preserve order while securing the plantation economy and maritime strategic routes.

era.zh: 殖民种植园与蒸汽航运时代

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与制度变化包括蒸汽船港口网络、电报联系、种植园管理、传教学校与殖民档案行政。
keyInnovations.en: Key technological and institutional changes included steamship port networks, telegraphic links, plantation management methods, missionary schools, and colonial record administration.

strengths.zh: 港口条件较好，出口经济潜力高，社会村社结构稳固，识字率和传教教育基础较强。
strengths.en: It had relatively good port conditions, strong export potential, resilient village social structures, and a solid base of literacy and missionary education.

weaknesses.zh: 政权合法性依赖殖民力量，内部对外来统治并非完全认同，经济过度依赖少数出口作物。
weaknesses.en: Its governing legitimacy depended on colonial power, internal acceptance of foreign rule was incomplete, and the economy was overly dependent on a few export crops.

outlook.zh: 短期内在德国治理下趋于稳定，但帝国竞争和本地民族政治迟早会挑战殖民秩序。
outlook.en: In the short term it was moving toward stability under German rule, but imperial rivalry and indigenous political aspirations would eventually challenge the colonial order.

description.zh: 1900年的萨摩亚是由德国殖民政府统治、以阿皮亚为港口核心并保留强大本地村社结构的南太平洋群岛社会。
description.en: In 1900, Samoa was a South Pacific island society ruled by a German colonial government, centered on the port of Apia while retaining strong indigenous village structures.

dynasty.zh: 诸酋长家族并立（如阿勒马克图姆、卡西米、努艾米等）
dynasty.en: Multiple ruling sheikhly houses (such as Al Maktoum, Al Qasimi, and Al Nuaimi)

governmentForm.zh: 特鲁西尔阿曼在1900年并非统一国家，而是受英国条约保护的多个世袭酋长国联盟式地区。
governmentForm.en: In 1900, Trucial Oman was not a unified state but a treaty-protected region of multiple hereditary sheikhdoms under British supervision.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以部落、氏族、海商家族与珍珠业社群为核心，游牧、绿洲农业和海岸贸易人口并存。统治结构依赖个人效忠、部落协商与宗教声望，而非高度官僚化的国家制度。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on tribes, clans, merchant families, and pearling communities, with nomadic, oasis-agricultural, and coastal trading populations coexisting. Rule depended on personal allegiance, tribal negotiation, and religious prestige rather than a highly bureaucratized state.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由各酋长家族、部落盟主、珍珠业与海贸精英以及宗教法学人士构成。沿海城镇中商人对政治影响尤为明显。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of sheikhly families, tribal leaders, pearling and maritime commercial elites, and religious jurists. In coastal towns, merchants exercised especially visible political influence.

succession.zh: 继承通常在统治家族内部按长幼、能力与部落支持协商决定，并非完全固定的嫡长制。英国在争位纠纷中常扮演仲裁或默认角色。
succession.en: Succession was usually decided within the ruling family through a mix of seniority, ability, and tribal backing rather than strict primogeniture. Britain often acted as an arbiter or tacit confirmer in disputes.

structure.zh: 各酋长国分别由酋长及其近臣、家族顾问和港口税务人员治理，没有统一中央官僚机构。英国政治代理通过海上停战条约影响对外事务与海上秩序。
structure.en: Each sheikhdom was governed separately by its ruler, close retainers, family advisers, and port tax officials, with no unified central bureaucracy. A British political agency shaped external affairs and maritime order through the Trucial treaties.

function.zh: 处理统治决策、部落关系、纠纷调停和外来使节往来。
function.en: Handled ruling decisions, tribal relations, dispute mediation, and dealings with outside envoys.

function.zh: 征收珍珠、贸易和停泊相关税费，管理港口秩序。
function.en: Collected taxes related to pearls, trade, and anchorage, and managed port order.

function.zh: 依伊斯兰法裁决婚姻、继承、债务与部分刑事案件。
function.en: Adjudicated marriage, inheritance, debt, and some criminal cases under Islamic law.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要依赖部落长老、绿洲首领、港口商人和酋长任命的代理人。内陆与海岸的行政密度差异很大，许多事务通过协商而非成文命令解决。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied mainly on tribal elders, oasis headmen, port merchants, and agents appointed by the sheikhs. Administrative density varied greatly between interior and coast, and many matters were resolved by negotiation rather than written decree.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以逊尼派伊斯兰法、部落习惯法和酋长裁断为主。涉及海上安全与对外关系的问题则受英方条约规范约束。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested primarily on Sunni Islamic law, tribal customary law, and the sheikh's judgment. Matters involving maritime security and foreign relations were constrained by British treaty arrangements.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以港口关税、珍珠业抽分、市场收费和贡纳为主，直接土地税较轻。财政收入随珍珠季和航运景气波动明显。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied on port customs, shares taken from the pearling industry, market fees, and tribute, while direct land taxation was light. Revenue fluctuated sharply with pearling seasons and shipping conditions.

religion.zh: 伊斯兰教
religion.en: Islam

culturalAchievements.zh: 该地区在口头诗歌、航海知识、珍珠潜水传统和伊斯兰法学教育方面具有鲜明特色。沿海城镇保留了连接印度洋与海湾贸易世界的海事文化。
culturalAchievements.en: The region was distinguished by oral poetry, navigational knowledge, pearling traditions, and Islamic legal learning. Its coastal towns preserved a maritime culture linking the Gulf with the wider Indian Ocean trading world.

languageFamily.zh: 闪米特语系阿拉伯语支
languageFamily.en: Semitic, Arabic branch

philosophy.zh: 文化观念以阿拉伯部落荣誉、伊斯兰伦理、海商信用与家族忠诚为核心。
philosophy.en: Cultural values were centered on Arab tribal honor, Islamic ethics, mercantile trust, and family loyalty.

writingSystem.zh: 阿拉伯字母书写阿拉伯语，商业记录多为简要账册与契约文书。
writingSystem.en: Arabic was written in the Arabic script, and commercial records were kept largely in concise ledgers and contract documents.

unit.zh: 印度卢比银本位等值/年
印度卢比银本位等值/人/年
unit.en: Indian rupees silver-equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以珍珠业、转口贸易、航海运输、椰枣农业和畜牧为基础。由于尚未进入石油时代，沿海财富高度依赖季节性珍珠收益与海湾贸易网络。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on pearling, re-export trade, maritime transport, date agriculture, and pastoralism. As the oil era had not yet begun, coastal wealth depended heavily on seasonal pearl income and Gulf trade networks.

mainIndustries.zh: 采珠、海运贸易、造船与修船、椰枣种植、畜牧、绿洲农业
mainIndustries.en: Pearling, maritime trade, shipbuilding and repair, date cultivation, pastoralism, and oasis agriculture

tradeGoods.zh: 出口天然珍珠、干鱼、椰枣、马匹少量；进口纺织品、木材、米粮、金属制品、茶糖
tradeGoods.en: Exports included natural pearls, dried fish, dates, and small numbers of horses; imports included textiles, timber, rice, metalware, tea, and sugar

name.zh: 印度卢比与玛丽亚·特蕾莎银元等并用
name.en: Indian rupee with Maria Theresa thalers and other silver coinage

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富表现为船只份额、珍珠潜水装备、牲畜、椰枣园与部落保护关系。沿海富户可积累银币和贸易债权，内陆家庭则更多持有牲畜与水源使用权。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed through shares in boats, pearling equipment, livestock, date groves, and tribal protection ties. Coastal elites could accumulate silver coin and trade credit, while inland households more often held livestock and water rights.

unit.zh: 印度卢比银本位等值/人/年
unit.en: Indian rupees silver-equivalent per person per year

unit.zh: 印度卢比银本位等值/年

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系为部落保护、商人信用和港口抽税相结合的海湾商业经济，市场交换与传统依附关系并存。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a Gulf commercial order combining tribal protection, merchant credit, and port taxation, with market exchange coexisting alongside traditional dependency ties.

unit.zh: 印度卢比银本位等值/年
印度卢比银本位等值/年
印度卢比银本位等值/年

treasuryDescription.zh: 各酋长国储备分散且规模有限，往往以银币、商人借贷和部落支持网络共同维持财政弹性。
treasuryDescription.en: Reserves in the sheikhdoms were dispersed and limited in size, with fiscal flexibility maintained through silver coin, merchant credit, and tribal support networks.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持酋长权威、港口运作和部落均衡，较少进行长期公共投资。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining sheikhly authority, port operations, and tribal balance, with comparatively little long-term public investment.

description.zh: 由酋长直属随从及忠诚部落战士组成，是主要地面武装。
description.en: Comprised the sheikh's direct retainers and loyal tribal fighters and formed the principal land force.

description.zh: 以武装帆船和贸易船改装舰只为主，用于护航、巡海和部落冲突。
description.en: Consisted mainly of armed sailing vessels and converted trading craft used for escort, patrol, and inter-tribal conflict.

description.zh: 危机时由各部落召集，战斗力取决于酋长号召力与装备来源。
description.en: Raised by tribes in times of crisis, with effectiveness depending on the sheikh's authority and access to arms.

commanderInChief.zh: 各酋长分别统帅本部武装
commanderInChief.en: Each sheikh commanded his own armed forces

technology.zh: 军事技术以步枪、旧式火炮少量、冷兵器和武装帆船为主。缺乏现代炮兵、铁路和统一补给体系，但海湾航海经验丰富。
technology.en: Military technology consisted mainly of rifles, a few older cannon, edged weapons, and armed sailing craft. It lacked modern artillery, railways, and a unified supply system, but possessed strong Gulf seafaring experience.

unit.zh: 印度卢比银本位等值/年

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自部落冲突、海上贸易纠纷、内陆势力竞争以及外部帝国对海湾航道的控制压力。
threats.en: The main threats came from tribal conflict, maritime trade disputes, inland rivalries, and external imperial pressure over Gulf sea lanes.

populationDescription.zh: 人口由阿拉伯沿海居民、贝都因部落成员、波斯湾海员、珍珠潜水者和少量波斯、印度商人组成。人口分布高度分散，沿海聚落与内陆绿洲之间联系依赖季节交通。
populationDescription.en: The population included Arab coastal residents, Bedouin tribal groups, Gulf sailors, pearl divers, and small numbers of Persian and Indian merchants. Settlement was highly dispersed, and links between coastal towns and inland oases depended on seasonal movement.

socialClasses.zh: 主要社会群体包括酋长家族、部落战士、商人、珍珠船主与潜水者、农牧民、宗教学者及外来商人。
socialClasses.en: Major social groups included ruling families, tribal warriors, merchants, pearling boat owners and divers, agricultural and pastoral communities, religious scholars, and foreign merchants.

allies.zh: 主要依赖英国在海湾的条约保护，并与阿曼、巴林及印度洋商港保持商业联系。
allies.en: The region relied chiefly on British treaty protection in the Gulf and maintained commercial ties with Oman, Bahrain, and Indian Ocean ports.

enemies.zh: 无统一国家层面的固定敌国，但常与邻近部落、海上竞争者及个别酋长国发生摩擦。
enemies.en: There was no single fixed enemy at the state level, but friction was common with neighboring tribes, maritime rivals, and individual sheikhdoms.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以遵守停战条约、维护海上贸易和在英国保护下保存各酋长自治为主。
foreignPolicy.en: External policy focused on observing the truce treaties, protecting maritime commerce, and preserving each sheikhdom's autonomy under British protection.

era.zh: 晚期帆船珍珠经济时代

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术在于传统航海术、珍珠采集组织、港口账册管理和与英印帝国蒸汽航线的间接接轨。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies and systems included traditional navigation, organized pearling operations, port ledger administration, and indirect integration with British Indian steamship routes.

strengths.zh: 海湾贸易位置优越，珍珠业收益可观，部落与商人网络灵活，英国保护降低外部海上打击风险。
strengths.en: It held an advantageous Gulf trade position, gained substantial pearling revenue, possessed flexible tribal and merchant networks, and benefited from British protection against major maritime attack.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一中央政权，财政分散，经济对珍珠周期依赖极高，内陆资源有限。
weaknesses.en: It lacked a unified central state, had fragmented finances, depended heavily on pearling cycles, and possessed limited inland resources.

outlook.zh: 1900年前后仍能维持稳定，但未来将易受珍珠市场波动、帝国干预和区域权力重组影响。
outlook.en: Around 1900 it could remain stable, but its future would be vulnerable to pearling-market shifts, imperial intervention, and regional realignment.

description.zh: 1900年的特鲁西尔阿曼是波斯湾沿岸由多个受英国条约保护的阿拉伯酋长国组成、以采珠与海贸为命脉的分散政治区域。
description.en: In 1900, Trucial Oman was a politically fragmented Gulf coastal region of Arab sheikhdoms under British treaty protection, sustained chiefly by pearling and maritime trade.

dynasty.zh: 法属苏丹殖民政权下的原图库洛尔国家遗绪
dynasty.en: Former Tukulor state under the colonial regime of French Sudan

governmentForm.zh: 1900年前后该地已并入法属苏丹，由法国殖民官员统治，并在地方上保留部分伊斯兰酋长与旧贵族作为附属中介。
governmentForm.en: By around 1900, the territory had been incorporated into French Sudan, ruled by French colonial officials while retaining some Muslim chiefs and former notables as subordinate intermediaries.

socialStructure.zh: 社会结构仍以穆斯林宗教精英、旧战士贵族、自由农牧民、手工业者与依附人口为层次。法国征服后，旧有圣战国家的军事贵族地位明显下降，但马拉布特与地方首领仍在乡村社会中保持影响力。奴役与附庸关系虽受殖民政权干预，仍未完全消失。
socialStructure.en: The social order remained layered among Muslim religious elites, former warrior nobles, free cultivators and herders, artisans, and dependent populations. After the French conquest, the status of the old military aristocracy declined sharply, though marabouts and local chiefs still retained influence in rural society. Slavery and dependency ties were weakened by colonial rule but had not entirely disappeared.

rulingClass.zh: 实际统治阶层是驻塞古及其他据点的法国殖民军政官员。地方层面则由受监督的酋长、卡迪与伊斯兰学者协助管理。
rulingClass.en: The effective ruling class consisted of French colonial military-administrative officers stationed in Ségou and other posts. At the local level, supervised chiefs, qadis, and Islamic scholars assisted in governance.

succession.zh: 主权不再依照图库洛尔哈里发国内部的世袭或宗教继承原则运作，而由法国殖民体系任命官员。地方酋长职位多在家族内部承袭，但须经殖民当局认可。
succession.en: Sovereignty no longer operated according to the hereditary or religious succession principles of the Tukulor Caliphate, but through appointments within the French colonial system. Local chiefships often remained within families, but required colonial approval.

structure.zh: 行政体系以法属苏丹总督区及塞古地方军政机构为核心，兼具军事占领与文官管理性质。法国官员控制税收、治安与司法上诉，而村社与酋邦事务多通过间接统治处理。伊斯兰法在婚姻、继承等领域保留有限空间。
structure.en: The administrative system centered on the territorial government of French Sudan and the local military-administrative apparatus at Ségou, combining occupation rule with civil administration. French officials controlled taxation, security, and judicial appeals, while village and chieftaincy affairs were often handled through indirect rule. Islamic law retained limited space in matters such as marriage and inheritance.

function.zh: 管理地方酋长、人口登记、征税与行政命令执行。
function.en: Managed local chiefs, population records, taxation, and enforcement of administrative orders.

function.zh: 负责驻军调动、巡逻、镇压抵抗与交通线保护。
function.en: Handled troop deployments, patrols, suppression of resistance, and protection of communication lines.

function.zh: 征收人头税、市场税、贸易税并管理库银。
function.en: Collected head taxes, market dues, trade taxes, and managed the treasury.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政主要依靠村长、宗族首领与被承认的酋长。殖民官员通过巡回监察、人质制度式约束与税务考核来控制地方。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied chiefly on village heads, lineage leaders, and recognized chiefs. Colonial officers controlled the countryside through itinerant inspections, quasi-hostage constraints, and tax-performance oversight.

legalSystem.zh: 刑事与政治案件由法国军政当局主导审理，重大案件可上诉至殖民法院。婚姻、继承和部分民事纠纷则常由伊斯兰法官按沙里亚与习惯法处理。
legalSystem.en: Criminal and political cases were mainly handled by the French military-administrative authorities, with major matters appealable to colonial courts. Marriage, inheritance, and some civil disputes were often dealt with by Islamic judges under sharia and customary law.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以人头税、市场税和贸易课税为主，并辅以劳役征发。实物征收与银币缴纳并存，税负常通过酋长体系向乡村转嫁。
taxationSystem.en: The tax system relied mainly on head taxes, market dues, and trade levies, supplemented by labor requisitions. Payment in kind coexisted with silver coin payments, and the burden was often passed down to rural communities through the chieftaincy system.

religion.zh: 伊斯兰教
religion.en: Islam

culturalAchievements.zh: 图库洛尔政权留下了清真寺、古兰经学校网络与伊斯兰法学传统。虽在1900年已失去独立，但宗教教育和口述史诗仍维持区域文化连续性。
culturalAchievements.en: The Tukulor state left behind mosques, networks of Qur'anic schools, and traditions of Islamic jurisprudence. Though no longer independent by 1900, religious education and oral epic traditions sustained regional cultural continuity.

languageFamily.zh: 尼日尔-刚果语系为主，富拉语、班巴拉语常用；宗教与学术语言为阿拉伯语。
languageFamily.en: Predominantly Niger-Congo languages, with Fula and Bambara widely used; Arabic served as the religious and scholarly language.

philosophy.zh: 以逊尼派伊斯兰、马立克学派法学与西非苏菲传统为思想核心，并混合富拉尼与班巴拉地方习俗。
philosophy.en: Its intellectual core was Sunni Islam, Maliki jurisprudence, and West African Sufi traditions, blended with Fulani and Bambara local customs.

writingSystem.zh: 阿拉伯字母阿贾米书写与阿拉伯文并用，殖民行政则 increasingly 使用法文与拉丁字母记录。
writingSystem.en: Ajami writing in Arabic script and Arabic itself were both used, while colonial administration increasingly employed French and the Latin alphabet for record keeping.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以粟米、高粱、稻作、牧业和区域集市贸易为基础，受法国军事征服与贸易重定向影响明显。沿尼日尔河的运输和税卡仍提供一定商业活力，但整体较前期圣战国家时代衰落。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on millet, sorghum, rice cultivation, pastoralism, and regional market trade, and was strongly affected by French conquest and the redirection of commerce. Transport and toll points along the Niger still provided some commercial activity, but the overall economy had declined from the era of the jihad state.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧业、河运贸易、皮革加工、纺织与小规模手工业
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, riverine trade, leatherworking, textiles, and small-scale handicrafts

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、牛羊、皮革、可乐果、盐、织物、奴隶余留交易品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, cattle and sheep, hides, kola nuts, salt, cloth, and residual goods linked to former slave trading networks

name.zh: 西非法郎前期混合币制
name.en: Pre-West African franc mixed currency system

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要以谷仓存粮、牲畜、农具和家族劳动力体现。富裕家庭则持有更多牛群、奴仆遗产关系与贸易联系。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was chiefly embodied in stored grain, livestock, tools, and family labor. Wealthier households held larger herds, inherited dependency relations, and broader trading connections.

unit.en: taels of silver per worker per year

economicSystem.zh: 属以村社农业和地区商路为基础、受殖民抽税与强制劳动影响的混合型经济。市场交换扩大，但国家主导的旧圣战财政体系已被法国殖民财政所替代。
economicSystem.en: It was a mixed economy based on village agriculture and regional trade routes, increasingly shaped by colonial taxation and forced labor. Market exchange expanded, but the old jihad-state fiscal system had been replaced by French colonial finance.

category.en: Judicial, religious affairs, and miscellaneous costs

treasuryDescription.zh: 地方库银规模有限，且高度依赖法属苏丹上级财政调拨。战争后秩序重建、运输困难与征收效率低下使财政长期偏紧。
treasuryDescription.en: The local treasury was limited in size and heavily dependent on allocations from the higher budget of French Sudan. Postwar reconstruction, transport difficulties, and weak collection efficiency kept finances chronically tight.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以稳定占领、扩大税基和降低直接驻军成本为主，故倾向依靠间接统治与劳役征发。殖民当局优先保障治安和运输，而非地方社会投资。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed primarily at stabilizing occupation, widening the tax base, and reducing the cost of direct military control, hence its reliance on indirect rule and labor requisition. Colonial authorities prioritized security and transport over local social investment.

description.zh: 由法国军官指挥的塞内加尔步兵与本地征募部队组成，是主要作战力量。
description.en: Composed of Senegalese tirailleurs and locally recruited troops under French officers, forming the main combat arm.

description.zh: 以轻骑与向导为主，用于巡逻、追击与交通线侦察。
description.en: Primarily light cavalry and guides used for patrol, pursuit, and reconnaissance along communication routes.

description.zh: 负责据点防卫、仓储看护与地方警备，战斗力较低。
description.en: Responsible for post defense, depot security, and local policing, with lower combat effectiveness.

commanderInChief.zh: 法属苏丹地区司令

technology.zh: 装备以栓动步枪、少量机关枪和野战炮为主，通信依赖信使、河运与有限电报线。相较本地旧政权军队，殖民军在火力、训练与后勤上占压倒优势。
technology.en: Equipment centered on bolt-action rifles, a small number of machine guns, and field artillery, while communications relied on couriers, river transport, and limited telegraph lines. Compared with the old local armies, the colonial forces held overwhelming advantages in firepower, training, and logistics.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自乡村零星反抗、跨境流动武装与征税引发的地方骚动，而非成规模国家战争。
threats.en: The main threats came from scattered rural resistance, armed cross-border movement, and local unrest provoked by taxation rather than large-scale interstate war.

populationDescription.zh: 人口以富拉尼、班巴拉、索宁克及其他尼日尔河流域社群为主，穆斯林占多数。战乱、掳掠遗绪与殖民重组使部分聚落人口下降，但河谷与集市仍保持相对密集居住。
populationDescription.en: The population consisted mainly of Fulani, Bambara, Soninke, and other Niger valley communities, with Muslims forming the majority. Warfare, the legacy of raiding, and colonial reorganization reduced some settlements, but the river valley and market centers remained relatively densely inhabited.

socialClasses.zh: 殖民官员与商人、伊斯兰学者与酋长、自由农牧民、工匠、依附人口与残余奴役群体
socialClasses.en: Colonial officials and merchants, Islamic scholars and chiefs, free cultivators and herders, artisans, dependent peoples, and residual enslaved groups

allies.zh: 正式上依附法国殖民体系，地方上与合作酋长和商人网络保持关系。
allies.en: Formally aligned with the French colonial system, and locally connected to cooperating chiefs and merchant networks.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象主要是反殖民抵抗者、越境武装团体及拒税社群。
enemies.en: Its opponents were chiefly anti-colonial resisters, armed cross-border groups, and tax-refusing communities.

foreignPolicy.zh: 地方并无独立外交，其对外政策由法国西非殖民战略决定，重点在边境安定、商路控制与防止泛伊斯兰政治动员。
foreignPolicy.en: The territory had no independent diplomacy; its external policy was determined by French West African colonial strategy, focusing on border pacification, control of trade routes, and the prevention of pan-Islamic political mobilization.

era.zh: 晚19世纪至20世纪初西非殖民军事行政时代
era.en: Late 19th- to early 20th-century era of colonial military administration in West Africa

keyInnovations.zh: 栓动步枪、机关枪、蒸汽河运、有限电报、殖民统计和税务登记制度
keyInnovations.en: Bolt-action rifles, machine guns, steam river transport, limited telegraphy, and colonial census and tax-registration systems

strengths.zh: 尼日尔河流域农业基础较稳固，伊斯兰教育网络与地方权威仍具社会整合作用。法国控制下的治安与河运改善了部分商业流通。
strengths.en: The Niger valley provided a relatively stable agricultural base, while Islamic educational networks and local authority structures still offered social cohesion. Under French control, security and river transport improved some commercial circulation.

weaknesses.zh: 政治自主性丧失，财政被殖民需要主导，旧贵族与乡村社会之间关系紧张。经济规模小且外向依附，易受征税、旱灾和交通中断影响。
weaknesses.en: Political autonomy had been lost, finances were subordinated to colonial needs, and tensions persisted between former elites and rural society. The economy was small and externally dependent, making it vulnerable to taxation, drought, and transport disruption.

outlook.zh: 短期内将继续被整合进法属西非体系，传统伊斯兰与酋长结构会在殖民框架下部分保留。长期看，地方身份和宗教网络仍可能成为反殖民政治的重要资源。
